If neither 'key_column' nor 'key_column_index' is provided, defaults to the first column (index 0).

inputs.name: sheetName
inputs.description: The name of the sheet/tab within the spreadsheet. Note: Google Sheets creates default sheets with localized names based on account language (e.g., 'Sheet1' for English, '工作表1' for Chinese, 'Hoja1' for Spanish, 'Feuille1' for French, 'Planilha1' for Portuguese, 'Лист1' for Russian). If you specify a common default name and the sheet is not found, the action will automatically use the first sheet if only one exists.

inputs.name: strictMode
inputs.description: Controls how rows with mismatched column counts are handled. When True (default), an error is returned if any row has more values than headers - the error message shows exactly which rows are affected and what values would need to be removed. When False, extra values are silently truncated to match the header count. Set to False only if you explicitly want automatic truncation of extra values.

inputs.name: tableStart
inputs.description: Cell where the table starts (where headers are located). Defaults to 'A1'. Use this if your table is offset (e.g., 'C5', 'D10').

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.description: The ID of the spreadsheet. Must be a non-empty string, typically a 44-character alphanumeric string found in the spreadsheet URL.

inputs.name: key_column_index
inputs.description: The 0-based column index to use as unique identifier for matching rows. Alternative to 'key_column' - will be converted to column name using headers. If neither 'key_column' nor 'key_column_index' is provided, defaults to 0 (first column). Example: 0 for first column, 1 for second column.

inputs.name: normalization_message
inputs.description: Internal field to track input normalization (e.g., row truncation). Not part of API.

name: Validate Events
description: Tool to validate Measurement Protocol events before sending them to production. Use when you need to verify event structure and parameters are correct before sending real data.

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: Required. Array of events to validate. Each event must have a 'name' field and optional 'params' object.

inputs.name: consent
inputs.description: Optional. The consent state for the user.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: Optional. A unique identifier for a user.

inputs.name: client_id
inputs.description: Required. Unique client identifier. This uniquely identifies a user instance of a web client.

inputs.name: api_secret
inputs.description: Required. The API secret from Google Analytics for the Measurement Protocol.

inputs.name: measurement_id
inputs.description: Required. The measurement ID for web streams. Format: G-XXXXXXXXXX where X is alphanumeric.

inputs.name: user_properties
inputs.description: Optional. The user properties for the measurement.

inputs.name: timestamp_micros
inputs.description: Optional. A Unix timestamp (in microseconds) for the time to associate with the event.

name: Get spreadsheet values
description: Returns a range of values from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to read data from specific cells or ranges in a Google Sheet.

inputs.name: range
inputs.description: The A1 notation or R1C1 notation of the range to retrieve values from. If the sheet name contains spaces or special characters, wrap the sheet name in single quotes (e.g., "'My Sheet'!A1:B2"). Without single quotes, the API will return a 400 error for sheet names with spaces. Examples: 'Sheet1!A1:B3', "'Sheet With Spaces'!A1:D5", 'A1:D5' (no sheet name uses first visible sheet), 'Sheet1!A:A' (entire column), 'SheetName' (entire sheet).

inputs.name: end_row
inputs.description: 1-based row number to stop reading at (inclusive). Use with start_row for pagination to avoid large response errors. Example: start_row=501, end_row=1000 fetches rows 501-1000.

inputs.name: start_row
inputs.description: 1-based row number to start reading from (inclusive). Use with end_row for pagination to avoid large response errors. Example: start_row=1, end_row=500 fetches the first 500 rows.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which to retrieve values. This is the long alphanumeric string found in the spreadsheet URL between '/d/' and '/edit' (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). WARNING: Do NOT use the spreadsheet name or title (e.g., 'My Sales Report'); you must use the actual ID from the URL.

inputs.name: major_dimension
inputs.description: The major dimension for results.

inputs.name: value_render_option
inputs.description: How values should be rendered in the output. FORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated and formatted (default). UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated but not formatted. FORMULA: Values are not calculated; the formula is returned instead.

inputs.name: date_time_render_option
inputs.description: How dates, times, and durations should be represented in the output. SERIAL_NUMBER: Dates are returned as serial numbers (default). FORMATTED_STRING: Dates returned as formatted strings.

name: Update spreadsheet values
description: Tool to set values in a range of a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to update or overwrite existing cell values in a specific range.

inputs.name: range
inputs.description: The A1 notation of the range to update values in (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:C2', 'MySheet!C:C', or 'A1:D5'). Must be actual cell references, not placeholder values. If the sheet name is omitted (e.g., 'A1:B2'), the operation applies to the first visible sheet. IMPORTANT: The range must not exceed the sheet's grid dimensions. By default, new sheets have 1000 rows and 26 columns (A-Z). If you need to write to columns beyond Z (e.g., AA, AB), first expand the sheet using GOOGLESHEETS_APPEND_DIMENSION or check the current dimensions using GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO.

inputs.name: values
inputs.description: The data to write. This is an array of arrays, the outer array representing all the data and each inner array representing a major dimension. Each item in the inner array corresponds with one cell.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet to update. This ID can be found in the URL of the spreadsheet (e.g., https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{spreadsheet_id}/edit). Must be a non-empty string.

inputs.name: major_dimension
inputs.description: The major dimension of the values. ROWS (default) means each inner array is a row of values. COLUMNS means each inner array is a column of values. Defaults to ROWS if unspecified.

inputs.name: auto_expand_sheet
inputs.description: If True (default), automatically expands the sheet's dimensions (adds columns/rows) when the target range exceeds the current grid limits. If False, the operation will fail with an error if the range exceeds grid limits.

inputs.name: value_input_option
inputs.description: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW: Values are stored exactly as entered, without parsing (dates, formulas, etc. remain as strings). USER_ENTERED: Values are parsed as if typed by a user (numbers stay numbers, strings prefixed with '=' become formulas, etc.).

inputs.name: include_values_in_response
inputs.description: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values.

inputs.name: response_value_render_option
inputs.description: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. FORMATTED_VALUE (default): Values are formatted as displayed in the UI. UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are unformatted (numbers, booleans, formulas). FORMULA: Formulas are not evaluated and remain as text.

inputs.name: response_datetime_render_option
inputs.description: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. Only used if includeValuesInResponse is true. SERIAL_NUMBER (default): Dates are returned as numbers. FORMATTED_STRING: Dates are returned as strings formatted per the cell's locale.

name: Watch Drive Changes
description: Tool to subscribe to changes for a user or shared drive in Google Drive. Use when you need to monitor a Google Drive for modifications and receive notifications at a specified webhook URL. Notifications may be batched rather than per-change; design handlers to be idempotent and fetch all changes since the last known page_token on each notification.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: A unique string that identifies this channel. UUIDs are recommended. Must be unique per active channel; reusing an ID can cause missed, delayed, or duplicate notifications.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of delivery mechanism for notifications.

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: An arbitrary string that will be delivered with each notification. Can be used for verification.

inputs.name: params
inputs.description:
Optional parameters for the notification channel.
Example: {"ttl": "3600"} for a 1-hour time-to-live (actual support depends on Google API).

inputs.name: spaces
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the corpora. Supported values are 'drive' and 'appDataFolder'.

inputs.name: address
inputs.description: The URL where notifications are to be delivered. Must be a publicly reachable HTTPS URL with a valid SSL certificate; HTTP, localhost, and private network endpoints are rejected by the API.

inputs.name: drive_id
inputs.description: The shared drive from which changes will be returned. If specified, change IDs will be specific to the shared drive.

inputs.name: page_size
inputs.description: The maximum number of changes to return per page.

inputs.name: expiration
inputs.description: Timestamp in milliseconds since the epoch for when the channel should expire. If not set, channel may not expire or have a default expiration. Channels are invalidated after expiry; re-establish the watch with a new channel before or after expiration to avoid missed changes.

inputs.name: page_token
inputs.description: The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This should be set to the value of 'nextPageToken' from the previous response or to the response from the getStartPageToken method. Persist this token per channel so change processing can resume correctly after restarts or interruptions.

inputs.name: include_labels
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

inputs.name: include_removed
inputs.description: Whether to include changes indicating that items have been removed from the list of changes (e.g., by deletion or loss of access).

inputs.name: supports_all_drives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Recommended to set to true if driveId is used or if interactions with shared drives are expected.

inputs.name: restrict_to_my_drive
inputs.description: Whether to restrict the results to changes inside the My Drive hierarchy. This omits changes to files like those in the Application Data folder or shared files not added to My Drive.

inputs.name: include_corpus_removals
inputs.description: Whether changes should include the file resource if the file is still accessible by the user at the time of the request, even when a file was removed from the list of changes.

inputs.name: include_permissions_for_view
inputs.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response.

inputs.name: include_items_from_all_drives
inputs.description: Whether both My Drive and shared drive items should be included in results.

name: Watch File for Changes
description: Tool to subscribe to push notifications for changes to a specific file. Use when you need to monitor a file for modifications and receive real-time notifications at a webhook URL.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this notification channel (max 64 characters).

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel.

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification for verification (max 256 characters).

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.description: The ID of the file to watch for changes.

inputs.name: params
inputs.description: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior.

inputs.name: address
inputs.description: The HTTPS address where notifications are delivered for this channel. Must have a valid SSL certificate.

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: Whether payload data should be included in notifications.

inputs.name: expiration
inputs.description: Date and time of notification channel expiration as Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Default: 3600 seconds, max: 86400 seconds for files.

inputs.name: includeLabels
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

inputs.name: acknowledgeAbuse
inputs.description: Whether the user is acknowledging the risk of downloading known malware or other abusive files. Only applicable to file owner/organizer.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.

inputs.name: supportsTeamDrives
inputs.description: Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.

inputs.name: includePermissionsForView
inputs.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response.

name: Abort Repository Migration
description: Tool to abort a repository migration that is queued or in progress. Use when you need to cancel an ongoing migration operation.

inputs.name: migrationId
inputs.description: The ID of the repository migration to abort. This is the migration ID returned when a migration was queued (e.g., 'RM_kgDOABCDEF').

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This can be used to track the mutation in logs or for idempotency purposes.

name: Add app access restrictions
description: Adds GitHub Apps to the list of apps allowed to push to a protected branch. The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled. This endpoint only works for organization repositories, not personal repositories. Apps must be installed on the repository with 'contents' write permissions.

inputs.name: apps
inputs.description: The list of GitHub App slugs with push access to grant. Apps must be installed on the repository and have 'contents' write permissions. The app slug is the URL-friendly name of the GitHub App (e.g., 'github-actions', 'dependabot').

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive. Note: This endpoint only works for organization-owned repositories.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch to which restrictions will be applied. Wildcard characters (e.g., '*') are not allowed in the branch name when using this REST API endpoint. For operations involving wildcard branch names, consult the GitHub GraphQL API.

name: Add a repository collaborator
description: Adds a GitHub user as a repository collaborator, or updates their permission if already a collaborator; `permission` applies to organization-owned repositories (personal ones default to 'push' and ignore this field), and an invitation may be created or permissions updated directly.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub handle for the user account to add as a collaborator. Cannot be the same as the repository owner, as owners are automatically collaborators.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Permission level for the collaborator. For organization-owned repositories, valid values are `pull` (read-only), `triage` (manage issues/PRs), `push` (read/write), `maintain` (manage most settings), `admin` (full control), or a custom repository role name if defined by the owning organization. This parameter is ignored for personal repositories, which grant `push` access.

name: Add assignees to an issue
description: Adds assignees to a GitHub issue. This action only adds users - it does not remove existing assignees. Changes are silently ignored if the authenticated user lacks push access to the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account or the organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.description: A list of GitHub usernames to add as assignees to the issue. Up to 10 assignees total can be on an issue. NOTE: This endpoint ADDS assignees - it does not replace existing ones. Users already assigned are kept. To remove assignees, use the 'remove_assignees_from_an_issue' action instead. The authenticated user must have push access to the repository; otherwise changes are silently ignored.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the issue within the repository. Must be a positive integer (GitHub issue numbers start at 1).

name: Add email for auth user
description: Adds one or more email addresses (which will be initially unverified) to the authenticated user's GitHub account; use this to associate new emails, noting an email verified for another account will error, while an existing email for the current user is accepted.

inputs.name: emails
inputs.description: Array of email addresses to add to the authenticated user account. Must contain at least one email address.

name: Add field to user project
description: Tool to add a custom field to a user-owned GitHub Projects V2 project. Use when you need to add fields like status, priority, or custom data to organize project items.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the field to create.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

inputs.name: data_type
inputs.description: The data type of the field. Use 'single_select' for dropdown fields, 'iteration' for sprint fields, or other types for simple fields.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's number.

inputs.name: single_select_options
inputs.description: Options for single select fields. Required when data_type is 'single_select'.

inputs.name: iteration_configuration
inputs.description: Configuration for iteration fields.

name: Add item to user project
description: Tool to add an issue or pull request to a user-owned GitHub project. Use when you need to add existing repository items to a project board.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the issue or pull request to add to the project. Required if owner, repo, and number are not provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The repository name. Required with owner and number if id is not provided.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of item to add to the project. Must be either Issue or PullRequest.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The repository owner login. Required with repo and number if id is not provided.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: The issue or pull request number. Required with owner and repo if id is not provided.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's number.

name: Add labels to an issue
description: Adds labels (provided in the request body) to a repository issue; labels that do not already exist are created.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: Array of label names to add to the issue. Labels that don't exist will be created automatically.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: Positive integer that uniquely identifies the issue within the repository. Must be >= 1.

name: Add org runner labels
description: Adds new custom labels to an existing self-hosted runner for an organization; existing labels are not removed, and duplicates are not added.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: A list of custom label names to add to the self-hosted runner. Previously applied labels are not removed.

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the self-hosted runner.

name: Add or update team membership for a user
description: Adds a GitHub user to a team or updates their role (member or maintainer), inviting them to the organization if not already a member; idempotent, returning current details if no change is made.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: The role to assign to the user within the team. A 'maintainer' has broader permissions than a 'member', such as managing team members and settings.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username of the user to add or whose membership to update.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The slug (URL-friendly version) of the team's name. Must be lowercase with hyphens instead of spaces (e.g., 'my-team' not 'My Team'). Spaces will be automatically converted to hyphens and text will be lowercased.

name: Add or update team project permissions
description: Adds a classic project to a team or updates the team's permission on it. This endpoint grants or updates permissions for a team on a specific classic project (not Projects V2). The authenticated user must have admin permissions for the project. Both the team and project must belong to the same organization. Requirements: - The project must be a classic project (not GitHub Projects V2) - The authenticated user must have admin permissions on the project - The team and project must be in the same organization - Requires 'admin:org' scope for the authentication token Returns HTTP 204 No Content on success, 403 if project is not an org project, or 404 if the organization, team, or project is not found.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (login) that owns both the team and the classic project. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The team's URL-friendly slug identifier within the organization. Found in the team's URL path.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Permission level to grant the team: 'read' (view only), 'write' (view and edit), or 'admin' (full control including settings). If omitted, uses the team's default permission.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: The unique numeric ID of the classic project. This is NOT the project number displayed in the UI.

name: Add or update team repository permissions
description: Sets or updates a team's permission level for a repository within an organization; the team must be a member of the organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Organization name where the team exists (case-insensitive). This is the organization that owns the team, not necessarily the repository owner.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive). This is the user or organization that owns the repository. In most cases, this should match the 'org' parameter value.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The team's slug (URL-friendly name).

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Permission level to grant the team. Valid values: 'pull' (read-only), 'triage' (manage issues/PRs), 'push' (read/write), 'maintain' (manage repo settings plus push), 'admin' (full control). Custom repository role names are also supported if defined by the organization.

name: Add a repository to an app installation
description: Adds a repository to a GitHub App installation, granting the app access; requires authenticated user to have admin rights for the repository and access to the installation.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the repository to add to the installation. The authenticated user must have admin permissions for this repository.

inputs.name: installation_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the GitHub App installation to which the repository will be added. This installation must be accessible to the authenticated user.

name: Add repo to org secret with selected access
description: Adds a repository to an existing organization-level GitHub Actions secret that is configured for 'selected' repository access.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the organization secret to which the repository will be added.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the repository to be granted access to the organization secret.

name: Add selected repo to org secret
description: Grants an existing repository access to an existing organization-level Dependabot secret when the secret's visibility is set to 'selected'; the repository must belong to the organization, and the call succeeds without change if access already exists.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization's unique handle/name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: Name of the Dependabot secret; must be alphanumeric or underscore, no spaces (lowercase auto-converts to uppercase).

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the repository to be granted access to the secret.

name: Add runner labels
description: Adds and appends custom labels to a self-hosted repository runner, which must be registered and active.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: Custom label names to add. Appended to existing labels. Names are case-insensitive. Empty list means no changes.

inputs.name: runner_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the self-hosted runner.

name: Add selected repository to an organization secret
description: Adds a repository to an organization secret's access list when the secret's visibility is 'selected'; this operation is idempotent.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the organization. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the secret. Secret names can only contain alphanumeric characters ([A-Z], [a-z], [0-9]) or underscores (_). Spaces are not allowed. Secret names cannot start with GITHUB_ and must be less than 255 characters.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the repository to add to the secret's access list.

name: Add selected repository to an organization variable
description: Grants a repository access to an organization-level GitHub Actions variable, if that variable's visibility is set to 'selected_repositories'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the existing organization-level GitHub Actions variable.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the repository to grant access.

name: Add a selected repository to a user secret
description: Grants a specified repository access to an authenticated user's existing Codespaces secret, enabling Codespaces created for that repository to use the secret.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: Name of the user-level Codespaces secret to which repository access is added; this secret must already exist for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the repository to be granted access to the specified user-level Codespaces secret.

name: Add social accounts for the authenticated user
description: Adds one or more social media links (which must be valid, full URLs for platforms supported by GitHub) to the authenticated user's public GitHub profile.

inputs.name: account_urls
inputs.description: Full URLs for social media profiles to add to the user's GitHub profile; these will be displayed publicly.

name: Add status check contexts
description: Adds status check contexts to a protected branch's required status checks. This action appends new status check contexts to the existing list of required status checks for a protected branch. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with status checks configured. Note: The 'contexts' parameter is deprecated by GitHub in favor of 'checks' array. For new implementations, consider using update_status_check_protection with the 'checks' parameter instead.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch to which status check contexts will be added. Branch names cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GraphQL API.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.description: The status check contexts to add to the protected branch. These are the names of CI/CD jobs or other checks that must pass before merging. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings. Examples: 'ci/test', 'linter', 'continuous-integration/travis-ci'.

name: Add sub-issue to an issue
description: Tool to add a sub-issue to a parent GitHub issue using GraphQL. Use when you need to establish a parent-child relationship between issues. Requires the 'sub_issues' GraphQL feature to be enabled.

inputs.name: issue_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the parent issue (e.g., 'I_kwDOABCDEFGH'). This is the GraphQL global identifier returned by GitHub's API.

inputs.name: sub_issue_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the sub-issue to link to the parent issue. Either sub_issue_id or sub_issue_url must be provided.

inputs.name: sub_issue_url
inputs.description: The URL of the sub-issue to link to the parent issue. Either sub_issue_id or sub_issue_url must be provided.

inputs.name: replace_parent
inputs.description: If true, replaces the existing parent issue if the sub-issue already has one. If false or omitted, the mutation will fail if the sub-issue already has a parent.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.

name: Add team access restrictions
description: Adds teams to the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. This action grants additional teams push access to a protected branch. Unlike 'Set team access restrictions' (PUT), this action adds to the existing list rather than replacing it entirely. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must exist in the organization and have repository access

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The organization name that owns the repository. Team access restrictions only work for organization-owned repositories, not personal repositories. This value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: teams
inputs.description: The list of team slugs to add push access to the protected branch. A team slug is the URL-friendly version of the team name (lowercase, hyphens instead of spaces). The teams must already have access to the repository. This adds teams to the existing list (does not replace the entire list).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the protected branch to add team restrictions to. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with restrictions configured. Wildcard characters are not supported. For wildcard branch protection, use the GitHub GraphQL API.

name: Add user access restrictions
description: Adds users to the list of people allowed to push to a protected branch in an organization repository. Important notes: - This action only works on organization-owned repositories (not personal repos) - The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled - Users must have write access to the repository to be added - The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (must be an organization for user restrictions). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: users
inputs.description: The list of user logins to grant push access to the protected branch. Users must have write access to the repository. An empty list will remove all user restrictions.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GitHub GraphQL API documentation.

name: Add users to codespaces access for an organization
description: Adds organization members to the list of users granted Codespaces access billed to the organization. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires the organization's Codespaces billing access to be set to 'selected_members' mode. If not already configured, use the GITHUB_MANAGE_ACCESS_CONTROL_FOR_ORGANIZATION_CODESPACES action first to set visibility to 'selected_members'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Organization's name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: selected_usernames
inputs.description: Usernames of organization members to be granted Codespaces access billed to the organization. These users must be members of the specified organization.

name: Approve a workflow run for a fork pull request
description: Approves a workflow run from a forked repository's pull request; call this when such a run requires manual approval due to workflow configuration.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the workflow run requiring approval.

name: Cancel sponsorship
description: Tool to cancel an active GitHub sponsorship using GraphQL. Use when you need to terminate a sponsorship relationship between a sponsor and a sponsorable entity. Either sponsor ID/login and sponsorable ID/login must be provided.

inputs.name: sponsor_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the user or organization acting as sponsor (e.g., 'U_kgDOABCDEF'). Required if sponsor_login is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsor_login
inputs.description: The username of the sponsoring user or organization. Required if sponsor_id is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsorable_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the user or organization receiving the sponsorship (e.g., 'U_kgDOMNOPQR'). Required if sponsorable_login is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsorable_login
inputs.description: The username of the sponsored user or organization. Required if sponsorable_id is not provided.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.

name: Cancel a workflow run
description: Cancels a workflow run in a GitHub repository if it is in a cancellable state (e.g., 'in_progress' or 'queued'). Returns 202 Accepted on success.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the workflow run.

name: Check if a user is a repository collaborator
description: Checks if a user is a collaborator on a specified GitHub repository, returning a 204 status if they are, or a 404 status if they are not or if the repository/user does not exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository, typically a GitHub username or an organization name. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username of the user whose collaborator status is being checked. This name is not case sensitive.

name: Check public organization membership
description: Tool to check if a user is a public member of an organization. Use when you need to verify public organization membership status.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

name: Clear Project V2 Item Field Value
description: Tool to clear the value of a field for an item in a GitHub Project V2. Use when you need to remove or reset a field value (text, number, date, assignees, labels, single-select, iteration, or milestone fields).

inputs.name: itemId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the project item to update (e.g., 'PVTI_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzgldG_w'). This identifies the specific item within the project whose field value will be cleared.

inputs.name: fieldId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the field to clear (e.g., 'PVTSSF_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzg8vTnA'). Currently supports text, number, date, assignees, labels, single-select, iteration and milestone fields.

inputs.name: projectId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the Project (e.g., 'PVT_kwHOCNqc1s4BN3oq'). This identifies the project containing the item.

name: Commit Multiple Files
description: Tool to atomically create, update, or delete multiple files in a GitHub repository as a single commit. Uses Git Data APIs to avoid SHA mismatch conflicts that occur with the Contents API when multiple files are modified in parallel. Use when you need to make multi-file changes reliably. BRANCH CREATION: When committing to a new branch (e.g., 'fix/my-fix' or 'feature/new-feature'), you MUST provide 'base_branch' (typically 'main' or 'master') to create the branch from. If the branch already exists, base_branch is not needed. This action handles race conditions automatically: if the branch is updated by another commit between fetching the HEAD and updating the reference (resulting in a 422 'not a fast forward' error), the action will retry by refetching the HEAD and rebasing changes. Use max_retries to control this behavior.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name (without .git extension)

inputs.name: force
inputs.description: Force update the branch reference. WARNING: Use with caution as this can overwrite commits. Only use this when you intentionally want to overwrite the branch history.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Repository owner (username or organization name)

inputs.name: author
inputs.description: Git author or committer information.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Target branch name to commit to. If this branch doesn't exist, you MUST provide 'base_branch' to create it. For new feature branches (e.g., 'fix/my-fix', 'feature/new-feature'), always set base_branch='main' (or your default branch).

inputs.name: deletes
inputs.description: List of file paths to delete from the repository. Files must exist in the repository; attempting to delete non-existent files will result in an error. Ensure paths are exact with no leading/trailing whitespace.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Commit message describing the changes

inputs.name: upserts
inputs.description: List of files to create or update. Each entry requires path, content, and optional encoding (utf-8 or base64)

inputs.name: committer
inputs.description: Git author or committer information.

inputs.name: base_branch
inputs.description: REQUIRED when committing to a new branch that doesn't exist yet. Specifies the existing branch to create the new branch from (e.g., 'main' or 'master'). Not needed if 'branch' already exists in the repository.

inputs.name: max_retries
inputs.description: Maximum number of retries when encountering race conditions (422 'not a fast forward' errors). The action will refetch the HEAD and rebase changes on each retry. Set to 0 to disable retries.

name: Compare two commits
description: Compares two commit points (commits, branches, tags, or SHAs) within a repository or across forks, using `BASE...HEAD` or `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` format for the `basehead` parameter.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of the results to fetch, for pagination when the number of differences (commits or files) is large.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: basehead
inputs.description: A string specifying the base and head references to compare, in the format `BASE...HEAD`. `BASE` and `HEAD` can be branch names (e.g., `main...develop`), tag names (e.g., `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`), or commit SHAs (abbreviated or full 40-character format). Both abbreviated and full SHAs are accepted; full 40-character SHAs are recommended for reliability in large repositories. Both references must exist in the specified repository. To compare branches across forks in the same network, use the format `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` (e.g., `octocat:main...another-user:main`). Do NOT pass GitHub URLs - only plain reference strings like 'main...develop' are accepted.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results (e.g., files or commits) to return per page, with a maximum of 100, for pagination.

name: Create a blob
description: Creates a Git blob in a repository, requiring content and encoding ('utf-8' or 'base64'). Requires write access to the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and you must have write access to it.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive. You must have write access to the repository.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The content to be stored in the new blob.
examples: This is the raw content of the file.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: The encoding for the 'content'; "utf-8" or "base64".

name: Create a check run
description: Creates a new check run for a specific commit in a repository, used by external services to report status, detailed feedback, annotations, and images directly within the GitHub UI. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot create check runs.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the check to be displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, "Code Coverage" or "Linter".

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: The current status of the check run. Only GitHub Actions can set `status` to `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

inputs.name: actions
inputs.description: A list of up to three action objects defining interactive buttons displayed on GitHub after the check run completes. Each object needs a `label` (button text), `identifier` (unique ID for the action), and `description` (tooltip). Clicking a button sends a `check_run.requested_action` webhook to your app.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.description: The SHA of the commit for which the check run is created.

inputs.name: conclusion
inputs.description: **Required if `completed_at` is provided or if `status` is `completed`**. Specifies the final outcome of the check (e.g., 'success', 'failure'). Note: Setting `conclusion` automatically sets `status` to `completed`. GitHub alone can set a conclusion to `stale`.

inputs.name: started_at
inputs.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run began.

inputs.name: details_url
inputs.description: The URL of the integrator's site providing full details of the check. If omitted, the GitHub App's homepage is used. This URL is available from the check's UI.

inputs.name: external_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the check run on the integrator's system. This helps in correlating check runs between GitHub and the external system.

inputs.name: completed_at
inputs.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) indicating when the check run finished. **Required if `status` is `completed`**.

inputs.name: output__text
inputs.description: Detailed textual information about the check run's output. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

inputs.name: output__title
inputs.description: A descriptive title for the output of the check run, displayed in the GitHub UI. For example, 'Linter Scan Summary'.

inputs.name: output__images
inputs.description: A list of image objects to display in the check run's output in the GitHub UI. Each object requires `alt` (alternative text), `image_url` (URL of the image), and an optional `caption`.

inputs.name: output__summary
inputs.description: A summary of the check run's findings. Supports Markdown. **Maximum length**: 65,535 characters.

inputs.name: output__annotations
inputs.description: A list of annotation objects detailing issues at specific code locations. Annotations are visible in the 'Checks' and 'Files changed' tabs of a pull request. Each annotation object should specify `path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `annotation_level` ('notice', 'warning', or 'failure'), and `message`. Optional fields include `start_column`, `end_column`, `title`, and `raw_details`. Up to 50 annotations per API request; for more, use subsequent 'Update a check run' calls. GitHub Actions limits are 10 warnings and 10 errors per step.

name: Create a check suite
description: Creates a new check suite for a specific commit (`head_sha`) in an original repository (not a fork). IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token - OAuth tokens and classic personal access tokens cannot use this endpoint. GitHub dispatches a `check_suite` webhook event with the `requested` action upon success.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.description: SHA of the commit for which to create the check suite, typically the head commit of a branch.

name: Create a codespace for the authenticated user
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for the authenticated user, requiring a JSON request body with either `repository_id` (integer) or a `pull_request` object (containing `pull_request_number` (integer) and `repository_id` (integer)). This action allows users to set up a development environment quickly by creating a codespace from a specified repository or pull request.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Git ref (branch, tag, or commit SHA) to use for the codespace.

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: ID of the repository to create the codespace from.

name: Create a codespace from a pull request
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for an open pull request in a Codespaces-enabled repository, with options to customize its configuration.

inputs.name: geo
inputs.description: Geographic area for this codespace (e.g., `UsEast`, `EuropeWest`); supersedes `location`. If unspecified, typically IP-derived.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: machine
inputs.description: Machine type for this codespace (e.g., `standardLinux`). Varies by CPU, RAM, storage. Uses default if unspecified.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: Requested geographic location (e.g., `EastUs`, `WestEurope`). This parameter is deprecated; use `geo`. If unspecified, location may be IP-derived.

inputs.name: client_ip
inputs.description: Client's public IP address for location auto-detection if proxied and `geo` or `location` are not specified.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the pull request.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: Custom name for the codespace.

inputs.name: devcontainer_path
inputs.description: Path to a `devcontainer.json` file in the repository that defines the development environment.

inputs.name: working_directory
inputs.description: Working directory for the codespace upon opening. Defaults to the repository root if unspecified.

inputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
inputs.description: Minutes of inactivity after which the codespace is automatically stopped. A default timeout applies if unspecified.

inputs.name: retention_period_minutes
inputs.description: Retention period in minutes (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days) for a stopped codespace. 0 means immediate deletion.

inputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
inputs.description: Set to `true` to prevent GitHub from requesting additional repository permissions defined in `devcontainer.json`. Defaults to `false` (permissions will be requested).

name: Create a codespace in a repository
description: Creates a GitHub Codespace for the authenticated user in a specified repository, which must be accessible and use a valid `devcontainer.json` if `devcontainer_path` is specified.

inputs.name: geo
inputs.description: Geographic area for the codespace, replacing `location`; determined by user IP if not specified.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Git reference (e.g., branch name) for the codespace; uses repository's default branch if not provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name owning the repository; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: machine
inputs.description: Machine type (CPU, RAM, storage). See 'List available machine types for a repository' action for options; uses a default type if not specified.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: Requested AWS region (best-effort, may differ); determined by user IP if not provided. Deprecated: use `geo`.

inputs.name: client_ip
inputs.description: IP address to infer preferred region if `location` and `geo` are unspecified; useful for proxied requests.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: Custom display name for the codespace; a default name is generated if not provided.

inputs.name: devcontainer_path
inputs.description: Path to a `devcontainer.json` in the repository; uses `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` or `devcontainer.json` at root if not specified.

inputs.name: working_directory
inputs.description: Directory to use as default when codespace opens; uses repository root if not specified.

inputs.name: idle_timeout_minutes
inputs.description: Minutes of inactivity before codespace auto-stops; uses a default timeout if not specified.

inputs.name: retention_period_minutes
inputs.description: Minutes an idle (stopped) codespace is retained before auto-deletion (0-43200, i.e., up to 30 days); uses a default period if not specified.

inputs.name: multi_repo_permissions_opt_out
inputs.description: If `true`, opts out of granting multi-repository permissions from `devcontainer.json`. If unspecified or `false`, permissions are granted if requested.

name: Create a commit
description: Creates a new commit in a GitHub repository; the `tree` SHA and any `parents` SHAs must already exist in the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: tree
inputs.description: SHA of the tree object for this commit; must exist in the repository.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The commit message.

inputs.name: parents
inputs.description: SHAs of parent commits; all must exist in the repository. Omit or provide an empty list for a root commit.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.description: ASCII-armored detached PGP signature over the commit data. GitHub adds this to the `gpgsig` header if provided.

inputs.name: author__date
inputs.description: Timestamp (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) when the commit was authored.

inputs.name: author__name
inputs.description: Name of the commit's author. Defaults to authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: author__email
inputs.description: Email of the commit's author. Defaults to authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: committer__date
inputs.description: Timestamp (ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`) when the commit was committed. Defaults to `author_date` or current time.

inputs.name: committer__name
inputs.description: Name of the committer. Defaults to author's name or authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: committer__email
inputs.description: Email of the committer. Defaults to author's email or authenticated user if not provided.

name: Create a commit comment
description: Creates a comment on a specific commit, or on a specific line if `path` and `position` are provided.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The text content of the comment.

inputs.name: line
inputs.description: **Deprecated:** Use `position` instead. Line number in the file to comment on. `position` takes precedence if both are provided.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Relative path of the file to comment on. If specified, `position` is also required.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository.

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: Line number in the diff's patch to comment on, relative to the commit's changes. Required if `path` is specified.

inputs.name: commit_sha
inputs.description: The SHA identifier of the commit to comment on.

name: Create a commit status
description: Sets a commit's status (e.g., error, failure, pending, success from CI/CD) for a given SHA; max 1000 statuses per SHA/context.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: SHA hash of the commit.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: State of the commit status, indicating check outcome or phase.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Label to differentiate this status from other systems (e.g., 'ci/jenkins'); case-insensitive.

inputs.name: target_url
inputs.description: URL linking to the source of the status (e.g., CI build log), displayed in GitHub UI.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Human-readable description of the status, displayed in GitHub UI.

name: Create a custom organization role
description: Creates a custom role with defined permissions within a GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive) where the custom role will be created.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Unique name for the custom role.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Optional short description of the custom role's purpose or permissions.
examples: Grants read-only access to organization settings and member audit logs.

inputs.name: permissions
inputs.description: List of fine-grained permission strings defining actions granted by this role. Use the List Organization Fine-Grained Permissions action to get available permissions for your organization.

name: Create a deploy key
description: Creates a deploy key for a repository; the repository must exist and be accessible, and the provided key must be a valid public SSH key.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: The full public SSH key. Ensure this is the public key, not the private key.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: A descriptive name for the new deploy key. If not provided, a default name will be generated.

inputs.name: read_only
inputs.description: If `true`, the deploy key will only have read access. If `false` (default), the key will have read and write access. Write access grants significant privileges.

name: Create a deployment
description: Creates a GitHub deployment for an existing repository, targeting a specific ref (branch, tag, or SHA) that must also exist within the repository.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The ref to deploy. This can be a branch name, a tag name, or a commit SHA.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and the authenticated user must have write access to it.

inputs.name: task
inputs.description: Specifies a task to execute, e.g., `deploy` or `deploy:migrations`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive. The repository must exist and the authenticated user must have access to it.

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: A JSON string payload providing additional information that a deployment system might need, customizable based on the deployment process.

inputs.name: auto_merge
inputs.description: Attempts to automatically merge the default branch into the requested ref if the ref is behind the default branch. Set to `false` to disable.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the deployment. Maximum length of 1000 characters.
examples: Deploying new feature X to staging.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Name for the target deployment environment (e.g., `production`, `staging`, `qa`).

inputs.name: required_contexts
inputs.description: A list of status check contexts to verify against commit status checks before creating the deployment. If omitted or `null`, GitHub verifies all unique contexts. Pass an empty list `[]` to bypass all checks.

inputs.name: transient_environment
inputs.description: Specifies if the given environment is specific to this deployment and will no longer exist at some point in the future (e.g., a staging environment for a pull request). Set to `true` if the environment is temporary.

inputs.name: production_environment
inputs.description: Specifies if the given environment is one that end-users directly interact with.

name: Create a deployment branch policy
description: Creates a deployment branch or tag policy for an existing environment in a GitHub repository, using a Ruby File.fnmatch pattern (where `*` doesn't match `/`) to specify which branches or tags are deployable.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name pattern for deployable branches/tags (Ruby File.fnmatch syntax; `*` won't match `/`, e.g., `release/*/*` for `release/feature/login`). Refer to [Ruby File.fnmatch documentation](https://ruby-doc.org/core-2.5.1/File.html#method-c-fnmatch).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Whether the policy targets a Git branch or a tag.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: Name of the target environment; URL-encode special characters (e.g., `/` as `%2F`).

name: Create a deployment status
description: Creates a status for an existing deployment, updating its operational state, associated URLs, and description.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: State of the status. Setting a transient deployment to `inactive` shows it as `destroyed` in GitHub.

inputs.name: log_url
inputs.description: URL for deployment output. Preferred over `target_url`; setting this also sets `target_url` to the same value.

inputs.name: target_url
inputs.description: URL for deployment status updates or output. `log_url` is generally preferred.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Short description of the status (max 140 characters).

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Name for the target deployment environment (e.g., `production`, `staging`). If unset, uses environment from previous status or deployment.

inputs.name: auto_inactive
inputs.description: If true, sets prior successful, non-transient, non-production deployments in the same repository and environment to `inactive`.

inputs.name: deployment_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the deployment for which to create a status.

inputs.name: environment_url
inputs.description: URL for accessing the target deployment environment.

name: Create a discussion
description: Creates a new discussion post on a specific team's page within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the organization (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Main content of the discussion post, typically in markdown.
examples: Let's brainstorm ideas for the upcoming hackathon.
Please review the attached document regarding the new API.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Title of the discussion post.

inputs.name: private
inputs.description: If `true`, post is private (visible only to team members, org owners, and team maintainers); `false` makes it public to all organization members.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: URL-friendly version of the team name (slug).

name: Create a discussion comment
description: Creates a new comment on an existing team discussion within a GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The content of the discussion comment. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.
examples: Great point! I agree with this approach.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The slug (URL-friendly version) of the team name.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the discussion within the team.

name: Create a fork
description: Creates a fork of an accessible repository, optionally into a specific organization, with a new name, or copying only the default branch.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The desired name for the newly created fork. If not provided, the new fork will have the same name as the original repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: organization
inputs.description: The GitHub organization name to fork the repository into. If not specified, the fork will be created in the authenticated user's account.

inputs.name: default_branch_only
inputs.description: Specifies whether to fork only the default branch of the repository. If `True`, only the default branch is copied. If `False` or not specified, all branches are copied.

name: Create a gist
description: Creates a new gist on GitHub with provided files, an optional description, and public/secret visibility.

inputs.name: files
inputs.description: JSON string representing content for the gist's files. Dictionary where keys are filenames and values are objects, each having a `content` key with the file's raw string data.

inputs.name: public
inputs.description: Indicates if the gist is public (`true`) or secret (`false`).

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Optional description for the gist.

name: Create a gist comment
description: Creates a new comment on a specified GitHub gist.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The text of the comment to be posted on the gist.
examples: Thanks for sharing this code snippet.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the gist.

name: Create a GitHub App from a manifest
description: Use this action to complete the GitHub App Manifest flow (step 3 of 3) by exchanging the temporary authorization `code` for the app's full configuration, including credentials and private key. The code is provided via redirect URL after creating the app through GitHub's web interface and must be used within 1 hour.

inputs.name: code
inputs.description: The temporary authorization code provided by GitHub during the app manifest flow (received as a query parameter in the redirect URL). This single-use code must be exchanged within 1 hour of generation to retrieve the app's configuration including credentials and private key.

name: Create a github pages deployment
description: Creates a GitHub Pages deployment for a repository using a specified artifact and OIDC token, provided GitHub Pages is enabled and the artifact (containing static assets) is accessible.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: oidc_token
inputs.description: OIDC token from GitHub Actions for deployment authorization, typically from `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` with `id-token: write` permission.

inputs.name: artifact_id
inputs.description: Identifier of a GitHub Actions artifact (e.g., .zip or .tar) with static assets for deployment. Artifact must belong to the repository. Either this or `artifact_url` is required.

inputs.name: environment
inputs.description: Target environment for this GitHub Pages deployment. Defaults to `github-pages`.

inputs.name: artifact_url
inputs.description: URL of a GitHub Actions artifact (e.g., .zip or .tar) with static assets for deployment. Artifact must belong to the repository. Either this or `artifact_id` is required.

inputs.name: pages_build_version
inputs.description: Unique string identifying the build version for this deployment, typically the Git commit SHA. Defaults to `GITHUB_SHA`.

name: Create a github pages site
description: Configures or updates GitHub Pages for a repository, setting build type and source; ensure a Pages workflow exists for 'workflow' `build_type`, or `source_branch` exists for 'legacy' or unspecified `build_type`. This action allows users to effectively manage their GitHub Pages site settings, specifying how the site is built and where the source files are located.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: build_type
inputs.description: Build process for the Pages site: 'legacy' (traditional GitHub Pages build) or 'workflow' (GitHub Actions). If omitted and `source_branch` is provided, defaults to 'legacy'. When 'workflow', `source_branch` and `source_path` are ignored.

inputs.name: source__path
inputs.description: Directory within `source_branch` for site source files (e.g., '/' for root, '/docs'). Ignored if `build_type` is 'workflow'.

inputs.name: source__branch
inputs.description: The branch for GitHub Pages source (e.g., 'main', 'gh-pages'). Required if `build_type` is 'legacy' or not specified. It is ignored if `build_type` is 'workflow'.

name: Create a label
description: Creates a new label in a specified GitHub repository, provided the repository exists and the user has write permissions.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the label. Emoji can be added to label names, using either native emoji or colon-style markup. For example, typing `:strawberry:` will render the emoji ![:strawberry:](https://github.githubassets.com/images/icons/emoji/unicode/1f353.png ":strawberry:"). For a full list of available emoji and codes, see the "[Emoji cheat sheet](https://github.com/ikatyang/emoji-cheat-sheet)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: color
inputs.description: The hexadecimal color code for the label (e.g., `f29513`), without the leading `#`. Required parameter.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the label. Maximum 100 characters.

name: Create a milestone
description: Creates a milestone in a GitHub repository for tracking progress on issues or pull requests; requires repository existence and user write permissions.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: State of the milestone.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Title for the new milestone.

inputs.name: due_on
inputs.description: Due date for the milestone (ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`).

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Detailed description of the milestone.
examples: Tracking all issues and PRs for the upcoming v1.0 release.
Tasks for the third quarter sprint.

name: Create an autolink reference for a repository
description: Creates a repository autolink to automatically convert text references (e.g., 'TICKET-123') into hyperlinks, using a unique `key_prefix` and a `url_template` that includes `<num>`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This can be a username or an organization name. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: key_prefix
inputs.description: Prefix that triggers the autolink (e.g., 'JIRA-'). When followed by an identifier, it's converted into a link. Must be non-empty and not contain spaces.

inputs.name: url_template
inputs.description: URL template for the autolink, using `<num>` as a placeholder for the reference identifier (e.g., 'https://example.atlassian.net/browse/<num>'). Behavior of `<num>` is determined by `is_alphanumeric`.

inputs.name: is_alphanumeric
inputs.description: Specifies if the `<num>` placeholder in `url_template` matches alphanumeric characters (`A-Z` (case-insensitive), `0-9`, and `-`) or only numeric characters (`0-9`).

name: Create an environment variable
description: Creates an encrypted environment variable for a pre-existing environment within a GitHub repository; will fail if the variable name already exists.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the environment variable to create (e.g., `API_KEY`).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The value of the environment variable (e.g., `your_secret_token`). This value will be encrypted.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the environment for which to create the variable. The name must be URL encoded if it contains special characters (e.g., slashes `/` must be replaced with `%2F`).

name: Create an issue
description: Creates a new issue in a GitHub repository, requiring the repository to exist and have issues enabled; specific fields like assignees, milestone, or labels may require push access.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The detailed textual contents of the new issue.
examples: Detailed description of the bug with steps to reproduce.
I think adding a dark mode would improve user experience...

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive). The repository must exist, be accessible, and have issues enabled.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The GitHub account owner of the repository (case-insensitive). The repository must exist and be accessible to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title for the new issue.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: Array of label names to associate with this issue (generally case-insensitive). NOTE: Only users with push access can set labels; they are silently dropped otherwise. Pass an empty list to clear all labels.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.description: Login for the user to whom this issue should be assigned. NOTE: Only users with push access can set the assignee; it is silently dropped otherwise. **This field is deprecated in favor of `assignees`.**

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.description: GitHub login names for users to assign to this issue. NOTE: Only users with push access can set assignees; they are silently dropped otherwise.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.description: The ID of the milestone to associate this issue with (e.g., "5"). NOTE: Only users with push access can set the milestone; it is silently dropped otherwise.

name: Create an issue comment
description: Creates a new comment on an existing GitHub issue or pull request within the specified repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Comment content in GitHub Flavored Markdown.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository (without `.git` extension); not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (username or organization); not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: Number identifying the issue or pull request for the comment.

name: Create an organization project
description: Creates a new classic project board within a specified GitHub organization. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects (classic) REST API. The classic projects feature may be disabled in some organizations, and GitHub recommends migrating to Projects V2 (accessible via GraphQL API) for new projects. Requirements: - The authenticated user must be an organization member with project creation permissions - Classic projects must be enabled for the organization - Requires 'repo' and 'admin:org' or 'write:org' scopes

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the organization (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Body or description for the new project.
examples: Tasks and milestones for the Q1 roadmap.
Detailed plan and discussion for the website redesign project.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name for the new project.

name: Create an organization repository
description: Creates a new repository within a specified GitHub organization, with options for detailed configuration including visibility, features, merge strategies, initial commit, and templates.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the organization where the repository will be created (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the new repository.

inputs.name: private
inputs.description: Whether the repository is private. `visibility` takes precedence if both are set.

inputs.name: team_id
inputs.description: ID of the team to grant access to this repository within the organization.

inputs.name: has_wiki
inputs.description: Enable wiki for this repository.

inputs.name: homepage
inputs.description: URL for the repository's homepage.

inputs.name: auto_init
inputs.description: Create an initial commit with an empty README.

inputs.name: has_issues
inputs.description: Enable issues for this repository.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Repository visibility: 'public' (visible to everyone) or 'private' (visible to collaborators).

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Short description of the repository.
examples: A project to demonstrate awesome capabilities.

inputs.name: is_template
inputs.description: Make this repository a template repository.

inputs.name: has_projects
inputs.description: Enable projects for this repository. Fails if organization has disabled repository projects and this is true.

inputs.name: has_downloads
inputs.description: Enable downloads for this repository (deprecated).

inputs.name: allow_auto_merge
inputs.description: Allow auto-merge on pull requests.

inputs.name: license_template
inputs.description: License template keyword (e.g., "mit", "apache-2.0"). See [GitHub license documentation](https://docs.github.com/articles/licensing-a-repository/#searching-github-by-license-type) for options.

inputs.name: custom_properties
inputs.description: JSON string containing custom properties for the repository as a key-value object. Example: '{"project_lead": "octocat", "status": "alpha"}'

inputs.name: allow_merge_commit
inputs.description: Allow merging pull requests with a merge commit.

inputs.name: allow_rebase_merge
inputs.description: Allow rebase-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_squash_merge
inputs.description: Allow squash-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: gitignore_template
inputs.description: Name of the .gitignore template to apply (e.g., "Python", "Node"). Refer to the [GitHub gitignore template list](https://github.com/github/gitignore).

inputs.name: merge_commit_title
inputs.description: Default title for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'MERGE_MESSAGE'.

inputs.name: merge_commit_message
inputs.description: Default message for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE', 'PR_BODY', or 'BLANK'.

inputs.name: delete_branch_on_merge
