inputs.description: Automatically delete head branches when pull requests are merged. Requires organization owner privileges if true.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_title
inputs.description: Default title for squash merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE'.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_message
inputs.description: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.

inputs.name: use_squash_pr_title_as_default
inputs.description: DEPRECATED: Use `squash_merge_commit_title`. Default to pull request title for squash-merge commits.

name: Create an organization variable
description: Creates a new, uniquely named GitHub Actions variable for an organization, with configurable repository access visibility (all, private, or selected).

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the organization variable. Must be unique at the organization level.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The value of the organization variable.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Controls which repositories in the organization can access the variable. Accepted values are 'all' (all repositories), 'private' (only private repositories), or 'selected' (specific repositories designated by `selected_repository_ids`).

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.description: A list of repository integer IDs that can access this organization variable. This field is required and only applicable when `visibility` is set to 'selected'.

name: Create an organization webhook
description: Creates a webhook for a GitHub organization to deliver event notifications to a configured URL.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the webhook. Must be "web" for organization webhooks. Defaults to "web".

inputs.name: active
inputs.description: Boolean indicating if the webhook is active. `true` sends notifications, `false` does not. Defaults to `true`.

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: List of event names to trigger the webhook. Use `['*']` for all events. Defaults to `['push']` if not specified. See GitHub docs for event types.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.description: Required. The target URL for webhook payload delivery. This URL is a mandatory part of the webhook's `config` object and will receive POST requests for subscribed events.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.description: Optional secret for generating an HMAC hex digest for payload verification (X-Hub-Signature-256 header). If provided, this is used as the key.

inputs.name: config__password
inputs.description: Password for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it, corresponding to `config_username`.

inputs.name: config__username
inputs.description: Username for basic authentication if the webhook endpoint (`config_url`) requires it. Use only if your endpoint is protected by basic auth.

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.description: The media type for serializing webhook payloads. Supported: `json`, `form`. GitHub API defaults to `form` if this field is not set or is `None`.

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.description: Controls SSL certificate verification for `config_url`. '1' disables verification (not recommended), '0' enables. GitHub API defaults to '0' (verification enabled) if this field is not set or is `None`.

name: Create a pull request
description: Creates a pull request in a GitHub repository, requiring existing `base` and `head` branches; `title` or `issue` must be provided.

inputs.name: base
inputs.description: The name of the branch you want the changes pulled into. This must be an existing branch on the current (target) repository. You cannot submit a pull request to one repository that requests a merge to a base branch of another repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The detailed description or contents of the pull request.
examples: This PR introduces a new feature that does X, Y, and Z.
Fixes #42 by addressing the off-by-one error.

inputs.name: head
inputs.description: The name of the branch where your changes are implemented. IMPORTANT: This branch must already exist in the repository (or fork) before calling this action. You must first create the branch and push commits to it (using git or the 'Create a reference' action), then create the PR. For cross-repository pull requests, namespace `head` with the source owner and branch, like `username:branch`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.description: Indicates whether the pull request should be created as a draft. Draft pull requests cannot be merged until marked as ready for review.

inputs.name: issue
inputs.description: The number of an existing issue in the repository to convert into a pull request. If provided, the issue's title and body may be used for the pull request. Required if `title` is not specified.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title of the new pull request. Required unless `issue` is specified.

inputs.name: head_repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World') where the changes in the pull request were made. This field is required for cross-repository pull requests if both the source and target repositories are owned by the same organization but are different repositories.

inputs.name: maintainer_can_modify
inputs.description: Indicates whether maintainers of the upstream repository can modify the pull request. This is primarily relevant for pull requests originating from forks.

name: Create a reference
description: Creates a NEW Git reference (branch or tag) in a repository. IMPORTANT: This action ONLY creates NEW references - if the reference already exists, it will fail with a 422 'Reference already exists' error. To update an existing reference, use the 'Update a reference' (GITHUB_UPDATE_A_REFERENCE) action instead. The repository must not be empty prior to this operation.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Fully qualified reference to create (e.g., `refs/heads/master`, `refs/tags/v1.0.0`). Must start with `refs/` and contain at least two slashes.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: SHA-1 hash of an existing commit in the repository that the new reference will point to.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

name: Create a registration token for an organization
description: Generates a temporary (one-hour) registration token to add a new self-hosted runner to an organization for GitHub Actions.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Create a registration token for a repository
description: Generates a time-limited token required to register a new self-hosted runner with a specific repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account or the organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Create a release
description: Creates a release in a GitHub repository for a specified tag; the tag must be unique for published releases, and if a `discussion_category_name` is given, it must already exist.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: A detailed description of the release. Markdown formatting is supported.
examples: This release includes new features X, Y, and bug fixes for Z.

# New Features
- Feature A
- Feature B

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The title of the release. If omitted and `generate_release_notes` is false, it defaults to the `tag_name`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.description: Set to `true` to create an unpublished (draft) release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.description: The name of the tag for this release. This tag must be unique for published releases.

inputs.name: prerelease
inputs.description: Set to `true` to identify this release as a pre-release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: make_latest
inputs.description: Specifies if this release should be marked as the latest for the repository. Draft releases and prereleases cannot be set as 'latest'.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) from which the Git tag is created. Defaults to the repository's default branch if the tag specified in `tag_name` doesn't exist yet.

inputs.name: generate_release_notes
inputs.description: Set to `true` to automatically generate the release title (`name`) and description (`body`) from the commit history since the last release. Defaults to `false`.

inputs.name: discussion_category_name
inputs.description: If specified, a new discussion will be created in this category and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository's discussions.

name: Create a remove token for an organization
description: Generates a token, valid for one hour, to authenticate removing a self-hosted runner from an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The unique identifier or name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive. For example, if your organization's URL is github.com/my-org, 'my-org' is the value.

name: Create a remove token for a repository
description: Generates a temporary (one-hour validity) token required to unregister and remove a self-hosted runner from a repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository (not case-sensitive).

name: Create a reply for a review comment
description: Posts a reply to a specific review comment on a GitHub pull request, requiring the repository, pull request, and original comment to exist, and a non-empty reply body.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The text of the reply to the review comment.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the top-level review comment to which the reply is being made. This must be the ID of a top-level review comment, not a reply to that comment. Replies to replies are not supported.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The number that identifies the pull request.

name: Create a repository dispatch event
description: Triggers a GitHub Actions workflow or a webhook on a repository by creating a repository dispatch event, allowing programmatic triggering of workflows based on events outside of GitHub.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: event_type
inputs.description: Custom webhook event name (100 characters or fewer).

inputs.name: client_payload
inputs.description: JSON string containing extra information for the webhook event (max 10 top-level properties), passed to the triggered workflow or action. Example: '{"ref": "main", "sha": "abc123"}'

name: Create a repository for the authenticated user
description: Creates a new repository for the authenticated user, optionally within an organization if `team_id` is specified.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the repository. Must be unique within your account. Will be used in the repository URL (github.com/username/repo-name). Can only contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores, and periods.

inputs.name: private
inputs.description: Whether the repository is private (true) or public (false).

inputs.name: team_id
inputs.description: The ID of the team to be granted access. Only valid if creating in an organization.

inputs.name: has_wiki
inputs.description: Whether the wiki is enabled.

inputs.name: homepage
inputs.description: A URL with more information about the repository.

inputs.name: auto_init
inputs.description: Whether to initialize the repository with a README.

inputs.name: has_issues
inputs.description: Whether issues are enabled.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the repository.
examples: This is a project about X.

inputs.name: is_template
inputs.description: Whether this repository is a template repository.

inputs.name: has_projects
inputs.description: Whether projects are enabled.

inputs.name: has_downloads
inputs.description: Whether downloads are enabled (deprecated by GitHub and may not be configurable).

inputs.name: has_discussions
inputs.description: Whether discussions are enabled.

inputs.name: allow_auto_merge
inputs.description: Whether to allow auto-merge on pull requests.

inputs.name: license_template
inputs.description: SPDX license key identifier (lowercase). Examples: 'mit', 'apache-2.0', 'gpl-3.0'. Must be one of the GitHub-supported licenses.

inputs.name: allow_merge_commit
inputs.description: Whether to allow merging pull requests with a merge commit.

inputs.name: allow_rebase_merge
inputs.description: Whether to allow rebase-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_squash_merge
inputs.description: Whether to allow squash-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: gitignore_template
inputs.description: Desired .gitignore template (e.g., 'Python', 'Node').

inputs.name: merge_commit_title
inputs.description: The default title for a merge commit. `PR_TITLE` uses the pull request's title. `MERGE_MESSAGE` uses a classic title like 'Merge pull request #123 from branch-name'. Required when `merge_commit_message` is set. Valid combinations: (PR_TITLE, PR_BODY), (PR_TITLE, BLANK), (MERGE_MESSAGE, PR_TITLE).

inputs.name: merge_commit_message
inputs.description: The default message for a merge commit. `PR_BODY` uses the pull request's body. `PR_TITLE` uses the pull request's title. `BLANK` results in a blank commit message. Valid combinations with merge_commit_title: (PR_TITLE, PR_BODY), (PR_TITLE, BLANK), (MERGE_MESSAGE, PR_TITLE).

inputs.name: delete_branch_on_merge
inputs.description: Whether to automatically delete head branches when pull requests are merged.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_title
inputs.description: The default title for a squash merge commit. `PR_TITLE` uses the pull request's title. `COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE` uses the commit's title (if only one commit) or the pull request's title (if multiple commits).

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_message
inputs.description: The default message for a squash merge commit. `PR_BODY` uses the pull request's body. `COMMIT_MESSAGES` uses the branch's commit messages. `BLANK` results in a blank commit message.

name: Create a repository from an unpublished codespace
description: Publishes the specified codespace to a new repository, using the codespace's current state as the initial commit.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The desired name for the new repository to be created from the codespace. If omitted, GitHub might generate a name (e.g., based on the codespace name).

inputs.name: private
inputs.description: Specifies the visibility of the new repository. Set to `true` for a private repository, or `false` for a public one. Defaults to `false` (public).

inputs.name: codespace_name
inputs.description: The unique name of the codespace to be published as a new repository. This codespace must exist, be unpublished, and belong to the authenticated user.

name: Create a repository project
description: Creates a new GitHub Projects V2 project board linked to a specified repository. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub. The project is owned by the repository owner (user or organization) and linked to the specified repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: An optional detailed description for the project board. Note: GitHub Projects V2 does not support a body/description field during creation, so this field is accepted but not used.
examples: Tasks and milestones for the upcoming website redesign.
Product features and initiatives for the fourth quarter.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the project board to be created.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

name: Create a repository ruleset
description: Creates a uniquely named ruleset for a repository, defining rules for branches or tags with specified enforcement, conditions, and bypass actors.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Unique name for the ruleset.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: rules
inputs.description: Array of rule objects as a JSON string to be enforced. Each must have `type` and may have `parameters`. Refer to GitHub API docs for available rule types and parameters. Example: '[{"type": "creation"}, {"type": "deletion"}]'

inputs.name: target
inputs.description: The target of the ruleset, whether it applies to branches or tags.

inputs.name: enforcement
inputs.description: Enforcement level: `disabled` (ruleset is not enforced), `active` (ruleset is enforced), or `evaluate` (test rules before enforcing; GitHub Enterprise only).

inputs.name: bypass_actors
inputs.description: Actors that can bypass rules in this ruleset as a JSON string. Each actor object requires `actor_id`, `actor_type` (e.g., 'Team', 'Integration'), and `bypass_mode` (e.g., 'always'). Example: '[{"actor_id": 1, "actor_type": "Team", "bypass_mode": "always"}]'

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__exclude
inputs.description: Ref names or patterns to exclude. Condition fails if any pattern matches the ref name.

inputs.name: conditions__ref__name__include
inputs.description: Ref names/patterns to include. Condition passes if one pattern matches the ref name. Use `~DEFAULT_BRANCH` for default branch, `~ALL` for all branches.

name: Create a repository using a template
description: Creates a new repository from an existing template repository; the authenticated user must have access to the template and, if creating in an organization, repository creation permissions within it.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The desired name for the new repository to be created.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that will own the new repository. Defaults to the authenticated user if not provided.

inputs.name: private
inputs.description: Set to `true` to create a private repository, or `false` to create a public repository.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A brief summary or description for the new repository.
examples: A new microservice for processing widgets.

inputs.name: template_repo
inputs.description: The name of the template repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: template_owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the template repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: include_all_branches
inputs.description: Set to `true` to copy the directory structure and files from all branches of the template repository, not just the default branch.

name: Create a repository variable
description: Creates a new, unencrypted variable in a repository for GitHub Actions workflows; fails if a variable with the same name already exists.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the variable to create. Variable names are case-sensitive and can only contain alphanumeric characters ([a-z], [A-Z], [0-9]) or underscores (_). They cannot start with the `GITHUB_` prefix, and spaces are not allowed.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The value of the variable. This will be stored as a string.

name: Create a repository webhook
description: Creates a webhook for a specified repository; requires admin permissions on the repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the webhook. Use `web`. This parameter currently only accepts `web` and defaults to `web` if not provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: active
inputs.description: Determines if notifications are sent when the webhook is triggered. `true` sends notifications (active), `false` disables them (inactive).

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: List of events to trigger the webhook (e.g., `['push']`, `['issues']`). See GitHub documentation for 'webhook event payloads'.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.description: The URL to which webhook payloads will be delivered via POST for subscribed events.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.description: Optional secret string for HMAC hex digest in delivery signature headers, enhancing security. See GitHub's 'delivery signature headers' documentation for details.

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.description: Media type for serializing payloads: `json` or `form`. Defaults to `form` if not specified. `json` delivers JSON, `form` delivers URL-encoded data.

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.description: Determines SSL certificate verification for the payload URL: "0" to verify SSL (recommended), or "1" to skip. Defaults to "0" (verify SSL) if not provided.

name: Create a review comment for a pull request
description: Creates a review comment on a pull request's diff, targeting a specific line, range of lines, an entire file, or replying to an existing comment.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Text content of the review comment.

inputs.name: line
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE]** Line number in the diff for the comment. Required when using line-based mode (must be used with `side`). For multi-line comments, this is the last line of the range. Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Relative path of the file for the comment.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: side
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE]** Side of the diff (`LEFT` or `RIGHT`) for the comment. Required when using line-based mode with the `line` parameter. For multi-line comments, refers to the last line of the range. Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters. **IMPORTANT:** Use `LEFT` only for deletions that appear in red in the diff. Use `RIGHT` for additions (green) or unchanged context lines (white). Attempting to comment on LEFT side lines that are not visible deletions in the diff will result in a 'could not be resolved' error.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: **[DEPRECATED - Use line-based mode instead]** Position in the diff hunk for the comment. When using position mode, do not provide line, side, start_line, start_side, or in_reply_to parameters.

inputs.name: commit_id
inputs.description: SHA of the commit for the comment. Using an outdated `commit_id` can misplace the comment if lines change.

inputs.name: start_line
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE - MULTI-LINE]** First line in the diff for a multi-line comment. Required when creating a multi-line comment (must be used with `start_side`, `line`, and `side`). Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters.

inputs.name: start_side
inputs.description: **[LINE-BASED MODE - MULTI-LINE]** Starting side of the diff (`LEFT` or `RIGHT`) for a multi-line comment. Required when creating a multi-line comment (must be used with `start_line`, `line`, and `side`). Do not use with position or in_reply_to parameters. **IMPORTANT:** Use `LEFT` only for deletions that appear in red in the diff. Use `RIGHT` for additions (green) or unchanged context lines (white). Attempting to use LEFT for lines that are not visible deletions will result in a 'could not be resolved' error.

inputs.name: in_reply_to
inputs.description: **[REPLY MODE]** ID of an existing review comment to reply to. When using reply mode, do not provide position, line, side, start_line, or start_side parameters as they will be ignored.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: Identifier of the pull request.

name: Create a review for a pull request
description: Creates a pull request review, allowing approval, change requests, or comments; `body` is required if `event` is `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `COMMENT`, and omitting `event` makes the review `PENDING`.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Review's main body text; required if `event` is `REQUEST_CHANGES` or `COMMENT`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: Review action type (`APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, `COMMENT`); if omitted, review is `PENDING` and requires later submission.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: comments
inputs.description: Inline draft review comments. Each object requires `path` (relative file path) and `body` (comment text). Optionally, specify `line` (or `start_line` for multi-line), and `side` (or `start_side`) for diff location. `position` is deprecated; use `line`. Example: `[{'path': 'file.py', 'line': 10, 'body': 'Refactor this.'}]`

inputs.name: commit_id
inputs.description: SHA of the commit to review; defaults to the latest pull request commit. Outdated SHAs may lead to stale comments.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: Identifying number of the pull request.

name: Create a scoped access token
description: Exchanges a user-to-server token for a new, fine-grained scoped access token for a GitHub App, requiring `client_id`, `access_token`, either `target` or `target_id`, and at least one permission; for repository-specific scoping, provide either `repositories` (names) or `repository_ids` (IDs), but not both.

inputs.name: target
inputs.description: Username or organization name for scoping the new token. Required if `target_id` is not provided.

inputs.name: client_id
inputs.description: Client ID of the GitHub App.

inputs.name: target_id
inputs.description: ID of the user or organization for scoping the new token. Required if `target` is not provided.

inputs.name: access_token
inputs.description: User-to-server access token to be exchanged for a new scoped token.

inputs.name: repositories
inputs.description: Repository names to scope the new token to. Cannot be used if `repository_ids` is specified.

inputs.name: repository_ids
inputs.description: Repository IDs to scope the new token to. Cannot be used if `repositories` is specified.

inputs.name: permissions__pages
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Pages settings, statuses, configurations, builds, and creating new builds. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__checks
inputs.description: Permission for checks on code (e.g., CI statuses). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__issues
inputs.description: Permission for issues, comments, assignees, labels, and milestones. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__actions
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Actions workflows, workflow runs, and artifacts. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__members
inputs.description: Permission for organization members and teams. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__profile
inputs.description: Permission to manage profile settings for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__secrets
inputs.description: Permission for repository secrets (e.g., Actions secrets). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__contents
inputs.description: Permission for repository contents, commits, branches, downloads, releases, and merges. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__metadata
inputs.description: Read-only permission for repository metadata, search, and collaborator listing. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__packages
inputs.description: Permission for packages in GitHub Packages. Applies to target repositories or organization.

inputs.name: permissions__starring
inputs.description: Permission to list and manage repositories a user is starring. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__statuses
inputs.description: Permission for commit statuses. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__followers
inputs.description: Permission to manage followers for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__gpg__keys
inputs.description: Permission to view and manage GPG keys for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__workflows
inputs.description: Permission for updating GitHub Actions workflow files. Requires `contents: write`. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__codespaces
inputs.description: Permission for Codespaces. Applies to target user/organization if repositories are not specified, otherwise to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__deployments
inputs.description: Permission for deployments and deployment statuses. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__environments
inputs.description: Permission for repository environments. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__single__file
inputs.description: Permission to access specific files in targeted repositories. The file paths must be configured in the GitHub App settings.

inputs.name: permissions__administration
inputs.description: Permission for repository administration (settings, teams, collaborators). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__git__ssh__keys
inputs.description: Permission to manage Git SSH keys for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__pull__requests
inputs.description: Permission for pull requests, comments, assignees, labels, milestones, and merges. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__email__addresses
inputs.description: Permission to manage email addresses for the user account. Applies to target user.

inputs.name: permissions__security__events
inputs.description: Permission for security events (e.g., code scanning alerts). Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__repository__hooks
inputs.description: Permission for repository webhooks. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__team__discussions
inputs.description: Permission for team discussions and related comments. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__plan
inputs.description: Read-only permission for viewing an organization's billing plan. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__dependabot__secrets
inputs.description: Permission for Dependabot secrets for targeted repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__interaction__limits
inputs.description: Permission to view and manage interaction limits on a repository. Applies to target repositories or organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__hooks
inputs.description: Permission for organization webhooks. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__events
inputs.description: Read-only permission to view organization audit log events. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__repository__projects
inputs.description: Permission for repository projects, columns, and cards. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__secrets
inputs.description: Permission for organization-level secrets (e.g., Actions secrets). Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__vulnerability__alerts
inputs.description: Permission for Dependabot alerts. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__packages
inputs.description: Permission for organization-level GitHub Packages. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__projects
inputs.description: Permission for organization projects and projects beta (if available). Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__secret__scanning__alerts
inputs.description: Permission for secret scanning alerts. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__custom__roles
inputs.description: Permission for custom repository roles within an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__administration
inputs.description: Permission for administrative settings of an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__user__blocking
inputs.description: Permission for viewing and managing users blocked by the organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__repository__custom__properties
inputs.description: Permission to view and edit repository custom properties, if allowed by property definition. Applies to target repositories.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__custom__org__roles
inputs.description: Permission for custom organization roles. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__custom__properties
inputs.description: Permission for custom properties for an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__announcement__banners
inputs.description: Permission to view and manage organization announcement banners. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__self__hosted__runners
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Actions self-hosted runners for an organization. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__personal__access__tokens
inputs.description: Permission for fine-grained personal access tokens requested by organization members. Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__copilot__seat__management
inputs.description: Permission for GitHub Copilot seat assignments for organization members (beta). Applies to target organization.

inputs.name: permissions__organization__personal__access__token__requests
inputs.description: Permission for requests for fine-grained personal access tokens within an organization. Applies to target organization.

name: Create a dependency snapshot
description: Creates a snapshot of a repository's dependencies to populate the GitHub dependency graph and enable security alerts; `sha` must be a 40-character commit ID, `ref` a fully qualified Git reference (e.g., `refs/heads/main`), and `scanned` an ISO 8601 timestamp.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Git reference (fully qualified, e.g., `refs/heads/main`) of the repository branch/tag that triggered this snapshot.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: Commit SHA (maximum 40 characters) associated with this dependency snapshot.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without `.git` extension (name is not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (name is not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: job__id
inputs.description: Optional external ID of the job that generated this snapshot.

inputs.name: scanned
inputs.description: UTC timestamp (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) when the snapshot was taken.

inputs.name: version
inputs.description: Version of the dependency snapshot submission, typically of the manifest or lock file format.

inputs.name: metadata
inputs.description: Optional user-defined metadata as a JSON string for domain-specific information. Limited to 8 keys with scalar values (e.g., string, number, boolean). Example: '{"key1": "value1", "key2": 123}'

inputs.name: manifests
inputs.description: Optional collection of package manifests as a JSON string (e.g., package-lock.json, pom.xml). Keys are manifest file names; values detail dependencies. Example: '{"package-lock.json": {"name": "...", "resolved": {...}}}'

inputs.name: detector__url
inputs.description: Optional URL of the detector tool used.

inputs.name: detector__name
inputs.description: Optional name of the tool used to detect dependencies.

inputs.name: job__html__url
inputs.description: Optional URL for the job that generated this snapshot.

inputs.name: job__correlator
inputs.description: Optional string to group snapshots. For a given `job.correlator` and `detector.name`, only the 'latest' snapshot is current. Useful to distinguish detection runs (e.g., concatenating GITHUB_WORKFLOW and GITHUB_JOB).

inputs.name: detector__version
inputs.description: Optional version of the detector tool used.

name: Create a tag object
description: Creates an annotated Git tag object in a repository, pointing to an existing Git object (commit, tree, or blob) defined by its SHA and ensuring the `type` field correctly specifies the object's type.

inputs.name: tag
inputs.description: The name of the tag, typically a version identifier (e.g., "v0.0.1").

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of the Git object being tagged.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: object
inputs.description: The SHA of the Git object (commit, tree, or blob) that this tag is pointing to.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The message associated with the tag.

inputs.name: tagger__date
inputs.description: The timestamp for when this object was tagged. This should be in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.name: tagger__name
inputs.description: The name of the person creating the tag.

inputs.name: tagger__email
inputs.description: The email address of the person creating the tag.

name: Create a team
description: Creates a new team in an organization, optionally with maintainers, repositories, specific privacy, notification settings, or a parent team; if `parent_team_id` is given, `privacy` must be 'closed'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Organization name where the team will be created (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the new team.

inputs.name: privacy
inputs.description: Team privacy: 'secret' (visible to owners and members) or 'closed' (visible to all org members). Defaults to 'secret' for non-nested teams; must be 'closed' if `parent_team_id` is set.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: **Deprecated**. Default repository permission ('pull' for read access, 'push' for write access) for the team.

inputs.name: repo_names
inputs.description: Repository full names (e.g., "org-name/repo-name") for team access; repositories must exist and be accessible to the organization.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Team's purpose or focus.
examples: A team for frontend development projects.

inputs.name: maintainers
inputs.description: GitHub user handles of organization members to be team maintainers; users must be organization members.

inputs.name: parent_team_id
inputs.description: ID of an existing team to be the parent, creating a nested team. If provided, new team `privacy` must be 'closed'.

inputs.name: notification_setting
inputs.description: Notification settings for @mentions: 'notifications_enabled' (members notified) or 'notifications_disabled' (no one notified). Default: 'notifications_enabled'.

name: Create a temporary private fork
description: Creates a temporary private fork of the specified repository to address a security vulnerability, linking the fork to a GHSA ID that must be specifically associated with this repository; the fork may take up to 5 minutes to become accessible.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner (username or organization) of the repository; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: ghsa_id
inputs.description: Identifier for the GitHub Security Advisory (e.g., GHSA-rfv9-x7w6-39px) that the fork will address.

name: Create a tree
description: Creates a new Git tree object in a repository, defining file/directory structure by specifying tree entries, optionally building on a `base_tree` SHA; all provided SHAs must be valid.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: tree
inputs.description: Array of objects defining the tree structure. Each object (node) requires `path` (string), `mode` (string; e.g., '100644' file, '100755' executable, '040000' subdirectory/tree, '160000' submodule, '120000' symlink), and `type` (string: 'blob', 'tree', or 'commit'). For new/updated blobs, provide `content` (UTF-8 string). For existing objects or `tree`/`commit` types, provide `sha` (string). To delete, specify `path`, `mode`, `type`, and set `sha` to `null`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: base_tree
inputs.description: SHA1 of an existing Git tree object to use as a base. If provided, its entries are combined with `tree` entries, where `tree` entries overwrite those from `base_tree` with the same path. Typically the SHA1 of the latest commit's tree for new branch changes. If omitted, the new tree is built from `tree` entries; files from the parent commit's tree not in `tree` are treated as deleted.

name: Create a user project
description: Creates a new GitHub Projects V2 project board for the authenticated user to organize and track issues, pull requests, and notes. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects V1 REST API has been sunset by GitHub.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Optional description for the new project. Note: GitHub Projects V2 does not support a body/description field during creation, so this field is accepted but not used.
examples: A project to track Q3 deliverables.
Planning for the next product launch.
Collection of ideas and tasks for the website redesign.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name/title for the new project. This will be displayed as the project board title.

name: Create a workflow dispatch event
description: Manually triggers a GitHub Actions workflow identified by `workflow_id` at a given `ref`, if the workflow is configured to accept `workflow_dispatch` events.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Git reference (branch or tag) for the workflow.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, excluding the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Owner of the repository (username or organization, case-insensitive).

inputs.name: inputs
inputs.description: JSON string containing key-value inputs for the workflow, matching `on.workflow_dispatch.inputs` definitions (max 10); uses workflow defaults if omitted. Example: '{"actor": "mona", "environment": "production"}'

inputs.name: workflow_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the workflow: numeric ID or just the filename (e.g., 'main.yml'). If a full path like '.github/workflows/main.yml' is provided, the prefix is automatically stripped.

name: Create commit signature protection
description: Enables commit signature protection for a specified branch, requiring all new commits to be signed.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, use [the GraphQL API](https://docs.github.com/graphql).

name: Create a custom deployment protection rule on an environment
description: Enables a custom deployment protection rule for an existing environment in a repository by linking a configured GitHub App (via `integration_id`) to control deployments.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository where the environment exists. Do not include the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: integration_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the GitHub App that provides the custom deployment protection rule. This app must be installed and configured for the repository. Required - use GITHUB_LIST_ENVIRONMENT_CUSTOM_DEPLOYMENT_RULES to find available integration IDs for your environment.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the environment to which the protection rule will be applied. This name must be URL-encoded if it contains special characters (e.g., slashes `/` should be replaced with `%2F`).

name: Create draft item for user project
description: Creates a draft issue item in a user-owned GitHub ProjectsV2. Use when you need to add a new draft item to track ideas or tasks in a user's project.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The body content of the draft issue item to create in the project.
examples: This is a test draft item created via API to verify endpoint functionality
Detailed description of the draft item

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title of the draft issue item to create in the project.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's number.

name: Create inference chat completions
description: Tool to create chat completions using GitHub Models inference API. Use when you need to generate AI-powered text responses using models like GPT-4, Claude, or Llama through GitHub's inference endpoint.

inputs.name: seed
inputs.description: If specified, the system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result.

inputs.name: stop
inputs.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. The returned text will not contain the stop sequence.

inputs.name: model
inputs.description: ID of the specific model to use for the request. Format: {publisher}/{model_name}, e.g., openai/gpt-4o, anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet.

inputs.name: tools
inputs.description: A list of tools (functions) the model may call. The model will choose when and how to call these functions based on the conversation context.

inputs.name: top_p
inputs.description: An alternative to temperature called nucleus sampling. Range: [0.0, 1.0]. The model considers tokens with top_p probability mass.

inputs.name: stream
inputs.description: If true, partial message deltas will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available.

inputs.name: messages
inputs.description: The collection of context messages associated with this chat completion request. Each message must have a role (assistant, developer, system, or user) and content.

inputs.name: max_tokens
inputs.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion. The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model's context length.

inputs.name: modalities
inputs.description: List of supported modalities for this request. Supported values: text, audio.

inputs.name: temperature
inputs.description: Controls the apparent creativity of generated completions. Range: [0.0, 1.0]. Lower values make output more deterministic, higher values more random.

inputs.name: tool_choice
inputs.description: Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.

inputs.name: stream_options
inputs.description: Options for streaming responses.

inputs.name: response_format
inputs.description: Specifies the format of the model's output.

inputs.name: presence_penalty
inputs.description: Influences probability of generated tokens appearing based on whether they appear in the text so far. Range: [-2.0, 2.0]. Positive values increase likelihood of new topics.

inputs.name: frequency_penalty
inputs.description: Influences probability of generated tokens appearing based on their cumulative frequency in the text so far. Range: [-2.0, 2.0]. Positive values decrease likelihood of repetition.

name: Create inference embeddings
description: Tool to create inference embeddings using GitHub's AI models. Use when you need to convert text into vector representations for similarity search, clustering, or other ML tasks.

inputs.name: user
inputs.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help monitor and detect abuse.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of strings. Each string will be converted to an embedding vector.

inputs.name: model
inputs.description: ID of the specific model to use for the request. Format: {publisher}/{model_name}

inputs.name: dimensions
inputs.description: The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have. Must be supported by the model (max 2048).

inputs.name: encoding_format
inputs.description: Format for embeddings: 'float' (default) or 'base64'. Float returns arrays of numbers, base64 returns encoded strings.

name: Create issue type
description: Create a new issue type for a GitHub organization. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization administrator. OAuth tokens need admin:org scope.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the issue type.

inputs.name: color
inputs.description: The color of the issue type.

inputs.name: is_enabled
inputs.description: Whether the issue type is enabled at the organization level.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the issue type.

name: Create JIT runner config for repo
description: Generates a temporary Just-In-Time (JIT) configuration for a new self-hosted GitHub Actions runner for a repository; any specified non-default `runner_group_id` must be an existing runner group accessible by the repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The desired name for the new self-hosted runner.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: A list of custom labels to assign to the runner. These labels help in targeting specific runners for jobs. Minimum items: 1. Maximum items: 100.

inputs.name: work_folder
inputs.description: The working directory for the runner, relative to its installation path. This is where job execution will take place.

inputs.name: runner_group_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the runner group to which the new runner will be assigned. Use 0 for the default group or the ID of a specific runner group.

name: Create or update an environment
description: Creates a new environment or updates an existing one in a GitHub repository, allowing configuration of deployment protection rules such as wait timers and reviewers; ensure `environment_name` is URL-encoded if it contains special characters.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: reviewers
inputs.description: JSON string containing up to 6 users or teams allowed to review deployments. Reviewers need at least read access; one approval suffices. Each reviewer object requires `id` and `type` ('User' or 'Team'). Example: '[{"id": 4532, "type": "User"}]'

inputs.name: wait_timer
inputs.description: Time in minutes to delay a job after it's triggered (0-43,200, i.e., up to 30 days).

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: Name of the environment. URL-encode if it contains special characters (e.g., '/' becomes '%2F').

inputs.name: prevent_self_review
inputs.description: If true, prevents the user who triggered deployment from approving their own job.

inputs.name: deployment__branch__policy__protected__branches
inputs.description: If true, only branches with existing protection rules can deploy; `deployment_branch_policy_custom_branch_policies` must then be false.

inputs.name: deployment__branch__policy__custom__branch__policies
inputs.description: If true, deployment is restricted to branches matching custom name patterns; `deployment_branch_policy_protected_branches` must then be false.

name: Create or update an environment secret
description: Creates or updates an environment secret with an `encrypted_value` that was encrypted using the public key identified by `key_id` for the specified environment.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: ID of the public key used for encryption, obtained from the 'Get an environment public key' GitHub API endpoint.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: Name of the secret. Case-sensitive.

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Value of the secret, encrypted with LibSodium using the public key obtained from the 'Get an environment public key' GitHub API endpoint for the target environment.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: Name of the environment. Case-sensitive; URL-encode special characters (e.g., `/` becomes `%2F`).

name: Create or update an organization secret
description: Creates or updates an organization secret for GitHub Actions, requiring its value to be pre-encrypted via LibSodium using the organization's public key.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: ID of the public key (from 'Get an organization public key' endpoint) used for encryption. This is required for both creating and updating secrets.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Controls which repositories can access the secret. If 'selected', `selected_repository_ids` must be provided.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the secret.

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Value for the secret, pre-encrypted using LibSodium with the organization's public key (obtained from 'Get an organization public key' endpoint). This is required for both creating and updating secrets.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.description: List of repository IDs that can access this secret. Required and only applicable when `visibility` is 'selected'.

name: Create or update a repository secret
description: Creates or updates a GitHub Actions secret within a specific repository; use `encrypted_value` and `key_id` to set or change its value.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The repository owner's username or organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: ID of the public key used to encrypt `encrypted_value` (obtained with the public key). Required if `encrypted_value` is provided.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name for the secret being created or updated. Must not start with 'GITHUB_' prefix (reserved for GitHub internal secrets).

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Value for the secret, pre-encrypted with the repository's public key using LibSodium (public key is obtained via 'Get a repository public key' endpoint).

name: Create or update a secret for the authenticated user
description: Creates or updates a Codespaces secret for the authenticated user; `encrypted_value` must be encrypted with the public key (ID: `key_id`) from GitHub's 'Get public key for the authenticated user' endpoint.

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: ID of the public key (from GitHub's 'Get public key for the authenticated user' endpoint) used to encrypt `encrypted_value`. If not provided, it will be fetched automatically.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the secret. This name must be unique among the user's Codespaces secrets.

inputs.name: secret_value
inputs.description: The plaintext value for your secret. This will be automatically encrypted using the user's Codespaces public key before being sent to GitHub. If you already have an encrypted value, use the encrypted_value parameter instead.

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Secret's value, encrypted using [LibSodium](https://libsodium.gitbook.io/doc/bindings_for_other_languages) with the public key (see `key_id`) obtained from GitHub's 'Get public key for the authenticated user' endpoint.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.description: Repository IDs that can access this secret. If omitted or empty, accessible to all user's Codespaces-enabled repositories.

name: Create or update custom properties for an organization
description: Creates new custom property schemas or updates existing ones in bulk for a specified organization; each property definition must include `property_name` and `value_type`.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The case-insensitive organization name.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.description: Definitions for custom properties as a JSON string. Each must include a unique `property_name` (max 75 chars, no spaces) and `value_type`. Optional: `description`, `required`, `default_value`, `allowed_values` (for 'single_select' type).

name: Create or update a custom property for an organization
description: Creates a new custom property (name must be unique for creation) or updates an existing one for an organization to define metadata for its repositories.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization's unique identifier (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: If true, property must be set for all repositories and `default_value` is mandatory.

inputs.name: value_type
inputs.description: Data type of the custom property. Can be 'string' (free text), 'single_select' (single choice from list), 'multi_select' (multiple choices from list), 'true_false' (boolean), or 'url' (URL format).

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Explanation of the custom property's purpose.
examples: Tracks the current status of a project.

inputs.name: default_value
inputs.description: Default value if property isn't set. For `single_select` type, must be one of `allowed_values`. Can be a string for single values or an array for multi_select.

inputs.name: allowed_values
inputs.description: Ordered list of allowed values (up to 200). Required and non-empty if `value_type` is `single_select` or `multi_select`.

inputs.name: values_editable_by
inputs.description: Who can edit the values of the property. Can be 'org_actors' (organization actors only) or 'org_and_repo_actors' (organization and repository actors).

inputs.name: custom_property_name
inputs.description: Name of the custom property (case-sensitive).

name: Create or update file contents
description: Creates a new file or updates an existing file in a GitHub repository; SHA can be provided to validate file updates, automatically fetched if not provided.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: The blob SHA of the file being replaced. Automatically fetched if not provided. If a 409 conflict occurs (SHA mismatch due to concurrent modifications), the action will automatically retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff and jitter.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: The full path to the file in the repository, including the filename and extension. Do not include leading slashes.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository (not case sensitive, without the `.git` extension).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The branch name. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted. If a non-existent branch is specified, the action will automatically fall back to the repository's default branch. For empty repositories, omitting this parameter is recommended to let GitHub initialize with the repository's configured default branch.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The new file content. Can be provided as plain text (will be automatically encoded to Base64) or as Base64-encoded text. GitHub API requires Base64 encoding, which this action handles automatically.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The commit message for this file creation or update.

inputs.name: author__date
inputs.description: Author's timestamp (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to committer's date if author details are provided but date is not.

inputs.name: author__name
inputs.description: Author's name. If specified, `author_email` is also required. Defaults to committer details; if all committer/author details omitted, GitHub uses authenticated user.

inputs.name: author__email
inputs.description: Author's email. If specified, `author_name` is also required. Defaults to committer details; if all committer/author details omitted, GitHub uses authenticated user.

inputs.name: committer__date
inputs.description: Committer's commit timestamp (ISO 8601 format). Defaults to current time if committer details are provided but date is not.

inputs.name: committer__name
inputs.description: Committer's name. If specified, `committer_email` is also required. GitHub uses authenticated user if all committer/author details omitted.

inputs.name: committer__email
inputs.description: Committer's email. If specified, `committer_name` is also required. GitHub uses authenticated user if all committer/author details omitted.

name: Create or Update GitHub Pages Site
description: Tool to enable or update GitHub Pages configuration for a repository. Use when setting up GitHub Pages for static site deployment or modifying Pages settings like custom domains and HTTPS enforcement.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: cname
inputs.description: Custom domain for the Pages site (e.g., 'example.com'). Set to null to remove custom domain. Only used during updates.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization). Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: build_type
inputs.description: Build process type: 'legacy' for traditional GitHub Pages build from a branch, 'workflow' for GitHub Actions. When 'workflow', source_branch and source_path are ignored.

inputs.name: source_path
inputs.description: The directory path within source_branch containing the site files: '/' for root or '/docs' for docs folder. Ignored if build_type is 'workflow'.

inputs.name: source_branch
inputs.description: The repository branch to use for GitHub Pages source (e.g., 'main', 'gh-pages'). Required if build_type is 'legacy'. Ignored if build_type is 'workflow'.

inputs.name: https_enforced
inputs.description: Whether to enforce HTTPS for the Pages site. Only used during updates.

name: Create or update repo secret with encrypted value
description: Creates or updates a Dependabot secret in a repository using an `encrypted_value` (pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the repository's Dependabot public key) and its corresponding `key_id`. This action allows you to securely manage secrets for a given repository, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected during the update process. By providing the encrypted value alongside the key ID, you can seamlessly maintain the necessary credentials for your repository's operations.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: The ID of the public key that was used to encrypt the secret value. This ID is retrieved from GitHub along with the public key via the 'Get a repository public key' endpoint for Dependabot secrets. If not provided or if an outdated key_id is used, the action will automatically fetch the latest public key from GitHub and retry with the current key_id.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the Dependabot secret to create or update.

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Value for your secret. This value must be encrypted with LibSodium, using the public key retrieved from the GitHub API's 'Get a repository public key' endpoint for Dependabot secrets. The resulting encrypted string is typically Base64 encoded.

name: Create reaction for a commit comment
description: Creates an emoji reaction for a commit comment; if the user has already reacted with the same content, details of the existing reaction are returned.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the repository owner. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The [reaction type](https://docs.github.com/rest/reactions/reactions#about-reactions) to add to the commit comment.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the commit comment.

name: Create reaction for an issue
description: Creates a reaction for a specified issue within a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The reaction type to add to the issue. See the [GitHub documentation on reactions](https://docs.github.com/rest/reactions/reactions#about-reactions) for full details.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: The number that identifies the issue.

name: Create reaction for an issue comment
description: Creates a reaction for a specific comment on an issue within a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account that owns the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: Reaction content to add to the issue comment. Must be one of the values from `ContentEnm`.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the issue comment.

name: Create reaction for a pull request review comment
description: Adds a specified reaction to a pull request review comment within a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The reaction to add. See the [GitHub API documentation on reactions](https://docs.github.com/rest/reactions/reactions#about-reactions) for more context on reactions.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the pull request review comment to which the reaction will be added.

name: Create reaction for a release
description: Creates an emoji reaction for a specific, existing release in a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The type of reaction to add to the release.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier (ID) of the release to add a reaction to.

name: Create reaction for a team discussion
description: Creates a reaction for a team discussion within a GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The reaction content to add to the team discussion.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The slug (URL-friendly version) of the team name.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the team discussion.

name: Create reaction for a team discussion comment
description: Adds a reaction to a team discussion comment, requiring the specified organization, team, discussion, and comment to exist.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name on GitHub. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: The type of reaction to add to the team discussion comment. For more information on reaction types, see the [GitHub API documentation on reactions](https://docs.github.com/rest/reactions/reactions#about-reactions).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The slug (URL-friendly version) of the team name.

inputs.name: comment_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the comment within the discussion.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the team discussion.

name: Create sponsorship
description: Tool to start a new GitHub sponsorship or reactivate a past sponsorship using GraphQL. Use when you need to sponsor a maintainer through GitHub Sponsors with a specified amount and privacy level.

inputs.name: amount
inputs.description: The amount to sponsor in cents (USD). For example, 500 cents equals $5.00.

inputs.name: sponsor_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the user or organization acting as sponsor (e.g., 'U_kgDOABCDEF'). Required if sponsor_login is not provided.

inputs.name: is_recurring
inputs.description: Whether the sponsorship is recurring (true) or one-time (false). Note: one-time payments currently have a known bug in the GitHub API and may not work as expected.

inputs.name: privacy_level
inputs.description: Privacy level for GitHub sponsorships

inputs.name: sponsor_login
inputs.description: The username of the sponsoring user or organization. Required if sponsor_id is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsorable_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the user or organization to sponsor (e.g., 'U_kgDOMNOPQR'). Required if sponsorable_login is not provided.

inputs.name: sponsorable_login
inputs.description: The username of the user or organization to sponsor. Required if sponsorable_id is not provided.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.

name: Create Sponsors Tier
description: Tool to create a new payment tier for your GitHub Sponsors profile using GraphQL. Use when you need to add a new sponsorship tier with a specific monthly price and description. The amount is specified in dollars and will be converted to cents automatically.

inputs.name: amount
inputs.description: The monthly sponsorship amount in dollars (USD). This value will be converted to cents internally (e.g., 5 for $5/month becomes 500 cents, 500 for $500/month becomes 50000 cents). Minimum is $1.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A detailed description of what this sponsorship tier provides. Explain the benefits and perks sponsors will receive at this level. This is a required field.
examples: Get access to exclusive content and updates
Priority support and early access to new features

inputs.name: sponsorable_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the user or organization who will receive sponsorships for this tier (e.g., 'U_kgDOABCDEF' for users, 'O_kgDOGHIJKL' for organizations). Use the get_viewer_graphql_id action to fetch your own ID.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations and track request origin.

name: Create or Update Organization Secret with LibSodium
description: Creates or updates a Dependabot organization secret, requiring the secret value to be pre-encrypted with LibSodium using the organization's public key obtained from the 'Get an organization public key' endpoint.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: key_id
inputs.description: ID of the Dependabot public key used for encryption (retrieved from 'Get an organization public key for Dependabot' endpoint: GET /orgs/{org}/dependabot/secrets/public-key). Required if `encrypted_value` is provided.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Secret visibility: `all` (all repositories), `private` (private repositories), or `selected` (specific repositories designated by `selected_repository_ids`).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The secret name (case-insensitive and unique within the organization).

inputs.name: encrypted_value
inputs.description: Value of the secret, encrypted using [LibSodium](https://libsodium.gitbook.io/doc/bindings_for_other_languages) and the organization's Dependabot public key (obtained from the 'Get an organization public key for Dependabot' endpoint: GET /orgs/{org}/dependabot/secrets/public-key). Required to create or update the secret's value.

inputs.name: selected_repository_ids
inputs.description: List of repository IDs that can access the secret; required and used only when `visibility` is `selected`.

name: Create user list
description: Tool to create a new user list on GitHub. Use when you need to organize and group GitHub users for easier tracking and management.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the user list to create.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A description for the user list to provide context about its purpose.
examples: A list of contributors I follow regularly

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This can be used to track the request.

name: Create view for user project
description: Tool to create a new view in a user-owned GitHub project (Projects V2). Use when you need to add a customized view with specific layout, filters, and visible fields to organize project items. Note: This action works with GitHub Projects V2. The view can be configured with different layouts (table, board, roadmap) and optional filters. The visible_fields parameter is not applicable to roadmap layouts.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the view to be created.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: The filter query for the view. See GitHub's filtering projects documentation for syntax details.

inputs.name: layout
inputs.description: The layout type for the view. Options are 'table', 'board', or 'roadmap'.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project. This can be the username or user login.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's number as displayed in the project URL.

inputs.name: visible_fields
inputs.description: List of field IDs that should be visible in the view. Not applicable to roadmap layout. If not provided, default visible fields will be used.

name: Delete a reference
description: Deletes a Git reference from a repository; 'ref' must be fully qualified (e.g., 'refs/heads/branch' or 'refs/tags/tag').

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The Git reference to delete. Accepts multiple formats: 'refs/heads/branch-name', 'heads/branch-name', or just 'branch-name' for branches; 'refs/tags/tag-name' or 'tags/tag-name' for tags. Simple branch names are automatically prefixed with 'heads/'.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

name: Delete a release

name: Delete a repository
description: Permanently deletes the specified repository; this is a destructive, irreversible action that requires admin privileges for the repository.

inputs.name: repo

inputs.name: owner

name: Delete a repository secret

name: Delete a repository variable
description: Deletes a named variable (e.g., for GitHub Actions workflows) from a repository; the repository and the variable must already exist.

description: Deletes a specific webhook from a repository.

name: Delete a review comment for a pull request
description: Deletes a specific pull request review comment.

description: Deletes a GitHub artifact by its ID within a repository, typically resulting in an empty response (HTTP 204 No Content) on success.

name: Delete a team

name: Delete a workflow run
description: Deletes a specific workflow run from a repository.

inputs.name: owner

inputs.name: run_id

name: Delete a commit comment
description: Deletes a specific commit comment, identified by its `comment_id`, from the specified repository; the comment must exist.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: The unique numerical identifier of the commit comment to be deleted.

name: Delete a deployment
description: Permanently deletes a specified *inactive* deployment from a repository.

inputs.name: repo
