inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: deployment_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the deployment to delete.

name: Delete a gist
description: Permanently deletes an existing GitHub gist, specified by its `gist_id`; this action is destructive and cannot be undone.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the gist to be deleted.

name: Delete a label
description: Permanently removes an existing label from a repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the label to delete. Label names are case-insensitive. If the label name contains spaces or special characters, ensure it is correctly URL-encoded (e.g., 'help wanted' becomes 'help%20wanted').

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Must be provided together with 'owner' to identify the repository (e.g., for 'github.com/octocat/Hello-World', repo is 'Hello-World'). This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The username or organization name that owns the repository. GitHub repositories are identified by owner/repo (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World'), so both 'owner' and 'repo' must be provided together. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Delete a milestone
description: Deletes the specified milestone if it exists; this operation is idempotent, typically returning a 404 if the milestone is not found or already deleted.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: milestone_number
inputs.description: The unique number that identifies the milestone to be deleted.

name: Delete package version
description: Tool to delete a package version using GitHub GraphQL API. Use when you need to remove a specific version of a package by its global node ID.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the package version to delete (e.g., 'MDEyOlBhY2thZ2VWZXJzaW9uMQ=='). This is the GraphQL ID, not the numeric REST API ID.

name: Delete a project
description: Deletes the specified GitHub project (Projects V2) using the GraphQL API. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025 and is no longer available. The project is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. The user must have admin permissions for the project to delete it.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: The unique GraphQL node ID of the project to be deleted (e.g., 'PVT_kwDOBcMqx84ABdPx'). This is returned from project creation or listing operations.

name: Delete project item for user
description: Tool to delete a project item for a user in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to remove an item from a user's project. Requires the project item's GraphQL node ID (starts with 'PVTI_'). Uses the GitHub GraphQL API deleteProjectV2Item mutation.

inputs.name: item_id
inputs.description: The GraphQL node ID of the project item to delete (e.g. 'PVTI_lADOBCxhzs4A...').

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's number.

name: Delete User List
description: Tool to delete a GitHub user list using GraphQL. Use when you need to remove a user list from a GitHub account.

inputs.name: listId
inputs.description: The ID of the user list to delete. This is a GitHub global node ID for the list.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Dismiss a review for a pull request
description: Dismisses an APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED review on a pull request with a mandatory explanatory message. IMPORTANT: Only reviews in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state can be dismissed. Reviews in COMMENTED, PENDING, or already DISMISSED state will return a 422 error. To dismiss a review on a protected branch, you must be a repository admin or be authorized to dismiss pull request reviews.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The event type for dismissing the review. Must be set to DISMISS.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: The reason for dismissing the review. This is a required field.
examples: All concerns addressed by recent commits.
Review no longer applicable after refactoring

inputs.name: review_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier for the review to be dismissed. The review must be in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the pull request within the repository.

name: Download a repository archive ZIP
description: Downloads a repository's source code as a ZIP archive for a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA). IMPORTANT SIZE LIMITATION: This action may fail with 'payload too large' errors for large repositories due to platform size restrictions. If you encounter size limit issues, consider these alternatives: - Use GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT to fetch specific files or directories (also lists directory contents) - Clone the repository using git if you need the full codebase Best suited for: small to medium repositories, downloading specific tagged releases, or quick code inspection.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The Git reference for the archive. This can be a branch name, a tag name, or a commit SHA.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.

name: Download job logs for a workflow run
description: Downloads logs for a specific job in a GitHub Actions workflow run, contingent on the repository's existence and the job ID being valid and having produced logs.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., user or organization). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: job_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the job for which to download logs. You can obtain this from the 'List jobs for a workflow run' endpoint.

name: Download workflow run attempt logs
description: Downloads a ZIP archive of logs for a specific workflow run attempt. Returns a downloadable ZIP file containing logs for all jobs and steps in the workflow run.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the workflow run.

inputs.name: attempt_number
inputs.description: The attempt number of the workflow run.

name: Download workflow run logs
description: Downloads logs for a specific GitHub Actions workflow run as a ZIP archive containing log files for each job in the workflow.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository (e.g., 'Spoon-Knife') without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., 'octocat'). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the workflow run (e.g., 12345).

name: Export an SBOM for a repository
description: Exports the software bill of materials (SBOM) in SPDX JSON format for a repository, if its dependency graph is enabled and it has at least one commit.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Find Pull Requests
description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Filter by specific repository. Can be provided in 'owner/repo' format (e.g., 'facebook/react'), or as just the repo name when used with the owner parameter. When owner and repo are both provided and have the same value, they are combined as 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with owner, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The repository must exist and be accessible with your token. Prefer 'owner/repo' format; bare repo name without the `owner` parameter causes invalid repository format errors.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Field to sort results by. Set explicitly to 'updated' with `order=desc` when recency matters; default behavior may return best-match relevance ordering instead.

inputs.name: label
inputs.description: Filter by label name.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order (ascending or descending).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Filter by repository owner (user or organization). Can be used alone to search all repositories for an owner, or combined with repo parameter to specify owner and repo separately. When both owner and repo are provided, the search is scoped to 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with repo, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The owner must exist and repositories must be accessible with your token.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Optional search query for PR title, description, or commit messages. Supports GitHub search syntax including label filters (e.g., 'label:bug'), logical operators (AND, OR, NOT), and text search. If not provided, PRs will be listed based on other filter parameters. Type qualifiers (type:pr, is:pr, is:issue, type:issue) are automatically normalized. When using logical operators with qualifiers like labels, the query is automatically grouped for proper parsing. When omitting query, ensure at least one structured filter parameter is provided; passing no query and no filters may cause validation errors. Prefer dedicated parameters (`label`, `state`, `author`) over embedding qualifiers in the query string. Exact title matching is not supported; post-filter on title client-side if needed.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: Filter by PR state: 'open', 'closed', or 'all'.

inputs.name: author
inputs.description: Filter by PR author username. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.description: Filter by PR assignee username. Use '@me' for current user. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Filter by programming language.

inputs.name: mentions
inputs.description: Filter by username mentioned in PR. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total search results at ~1000 items; iterate `page` until results fewer than `per_page` are returned, and add `repo`, `label`, or date filters when totals approach 1000.

inputs.name: is_merged
inputs.description: Filter by merge status. True for merged PRs, False for unmerged, None for all.

inputs.name: base_branch
inputs.description: Filter by base branch name.

inputs.name: head_branch
inputs.description: Filter by head branch name.

inputs.name: created_since
inputs.description: Filter PRs created after this date (ISO 8601 format). Provide as UTC ISO 8601 timestamp to avoid off-by-one-day errors at range boundaries. Does not filter on `merged_at`.

inputs.name: updated_since
inputs.description: Filter PRs updated after this date (ISO 8601 format).

inputs.name: response_detail
inputs.description: Level of detail in the response. 'minimal' (default) returns an optimized response for AI agents. 'full' returns the complete GitHub API response.

inputs.name: for_authenticated_user
inputs.description: Include private repositories accessible to the authenticated user in search results. When True without repo/owner, searches PRs involving you (author, assignee, mentions, commenter) across all accessible repos. When False without repo/owner, restricts to public repos only. When repo/owner is specified, this flag has no effect on scope. Set to True when searching private repositories; False searches all of GitHub and produces large noisy result sets.

name: Find Repositories
description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Field to sort results by.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order (ascending or descending).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Filter by specific user or organization. Do NOT use this parameter if your 'query' already contains 'user:', 'org:', or 'repo:' qualifiers - they will conflict. Use either 'owner' parameter OR search qualifiers in 'query', not both.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Search query for repository name, description, or README content. Accepts both 'query' and 'q' as parameter names. Supports OR operator for alternatives (e.g., 'bug OR error'). Multi-word phrases in OR expressions are automatically quoted. GitHub limits OR expressions to 6 terms maximum; queries exceeding this limit are truncated. If using GitHub qualifiers like 'repo:', 'user:', or 'org:', they must be properly formatted (e.g., 'repo:owner/name', 'user:username', 'org:orgname'). Note: 'owner:' is not a valid GitHub qualifier; if used, it will be automatically transformed to 'user:'. Must be non-empty; blank strings trigger a 'Search query cannot be empty' validation error.

inputs.name: topic
inputs.description: Filter by repository topic/tag.

inputs.name: archived
inputs.description: Filter by archived status. None means include both.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Filter by programming language.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total results at ~1000 per query; for exhaustive searches, split by `language`, `topic`, `min_stars`/`max_stars`, or `owner`.

inputs.name: max_stars
inputs.description: Maximum number of stars the repository should have.

inputs.name: min_forks
inputs.description: Minimum number of forks the repository should have.

inputs.name: min_stars
inputs.description: Minimum number of stars the repository should have.

inputs.name: fork_filter
inputs.description: How to handle forks: 'include' (include forks), 'exclude' (exclude forks), 'only' (only forks).

inputs.name: response_detail
inputs.description: Level of detail in the response. 'minimal' (default) returns an optimized response for AI agents. 'full' returns the complete GitHub API response.

inputs.name: for_authenticated_user
inputs.description: Search only in repositories accessible to the authenticated user (including private repos). Requires token scopes `repo`/`read:org`; sparse results compared to GitHub UI typically indicate insufficient token scopes.

name: Follow Organization (GraphQL)
description: Tool to follow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow an organization.

inputs.name: organizationId
inputs.description: The global GraphQL node ID of the organization to follow. Must be a valid organization node ID.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Follow User (GraphQL)
description: Tool to follow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow another user.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.description: The global Node ID of the user to follow. Must be a valid GitHub user ID in the format 'MDQ6VXNlcjU4MzIzMQ=='.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Fork a gist
description: Forks a specified public gist, creating a copy under the authenticated user's account.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the gist to be forked.

name: Generate release notes content for a release
description: Generates Markdown release notes content (listing changes, pull requests, and contributors) for a GitHub repository release, customizable via tags and a configuration file.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.description: The tag name for which the release notes are to be generated. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., a branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) that will be the target for the release's tag. This is required if the `tag_name` does not reference an existing tag. It is ignored if `tag_name` already exists.

inputs.name: previous_tag_name
inputs.description: The name of the previous tag to use as the starting point for the release notes. This manually specifies the range of changes to be included. If omitted, GitHub will attempt to automatically determine the previous release.

inputs.name: configuration_file_path
inputs.description: Specifies a path to a configuration file in the repository (e.g., `.github/release.yml`) containing settings for generating release notes. If unspecified, GitHub looks for a configuration file at ".github/release.yml" or ".github/release.yaml". If no such file is found, default settings are used.

name: Get a blob
description: Retrieves the raw, typically Base64-encoded, content of a file (blob) from a GitHub repository using its SHA hash, if the repository and blob SHA exist.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: file_sha
inputs.description: The SHA-1 hash of the file (blob) to retrieve.

name: Get a branch
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specified branch within a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Cannot contain wildcard characters. To use wildcard characters in branch names, use [the GraphQL API](https://docs.github.com/graphql). If 'HEAD' is provided, it will be resolved to the repository's default branch.

name: Get access restrictions
description: Lists users, teams, and GitHub Apps with push access to a branch; this branch must be protected in repository settings for detailed restrictions, otherwise expect a 404 or empty response.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., username or organization name). This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Wildcard characters are not supported in this field. To manage branch protections with wildcard patterns, please refer to the GitHub GraphQL API documentation.

name: Get a commit
description: Retrieves a specific commit from a repository by its owner, name, and a valid commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag), supporting pagination for large diffs.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The commit reference. Can be a commit SHA (e.g., '`sha`'), a branch name (e.g., 'heads/`BRANCH_NAME`' or simply '`BRANCH_NAME`'), or a tag name (e.g., 'tags/`TAG_NAME`'). For more information, see "[Git References](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Internals-Git-References)" in the Git documentation.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number for paginating the commit's diff if it's too large. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page when paginating the commit's diff (max 100). For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

name: Get admin branch protection
description: Checks if repository administrators are subject to the branch protection rules on a specific branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Name of the branch (wildcard characters not allowed; use GraphQL API for wildcard support).

name: Get all API versions
description: Retrieves all officially supported, date-based (e.g., "2022-11-28") versions of the GitHub REST API from the /versions endpoint.

name: Get all contributor commit activity
description: Retrieves commit activity (total commits, weekly additions/deletions/commits) for all contributors to a repository; may require a retry if GitHub returns 202 while preparing data.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, excluding `.git` (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the repository owner (case-insensitive).

name: Get all deployment protection rules for an environment
description: Lists all enabled custom deployment protection rules for a specific environment in a repository; the environment must exist and be configured for deployments.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the environment. This name must be URL-encoded. For instance, if the environment name contains slashes (`/`), they should be replaced with `%2F`.

name: Get a milestone
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific milestone within a GitHub repository by its number.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: milestone_number
inputs.description: The unique sequential number identifying the milestone within the repository.

name: Get an app
description: Retrieves publicly available information for an existing GitHub App, identified by its unique URL-friendly `app_slug`.

inputs.name: app_slug
inputs.description: The app's unique, URL-friendly, lowercase, hyphenated identifier, derived from its name (e.g., 'my-awesome-app').

name: Get an artifact
description: Gets a specific artifact for a repository by `artifact_id`. This action retrieves the details of a specific artifact from a GitHub repository using the artifact's unique identifier. It allows users to access information about artifacts stored in the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: artifact_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier (ID) of the artifact to retrieve. This ID can be obtained from other API calls that list artifacts.

name: Get an assignment
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub Classroom assignment if the authenticated user is an administrator of the classroom.

inputs.name: assignment_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the GitHub Classroom assignment to retrieve.

name: Get an autolink reference of a repository
description: Retrieves a specific autolink reference (which automatically hyperlinks text like 'JIRA-123' to an external system) for a repository using its unique ID; requires administrator access to the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: autolink_id
inputs.description: The unique numeric identifier of the autolink configuration to retrieve. You can get this ID from the 'Get all autolinks of a repository' action.

name: Get an environment
description: Retrieves the details of a specific deployment environment for a given repository, including its name, configurations, and current status.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the deployment environment. The name must be URL-encoded; for example, any slashes `/` must be replaced with `%2F`.

name: Get an environment public key
description: Retrieves the public key for a specified GitHub repository environment, used to encrypt secrets for GitHub Actions.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes (`/`) in the name must be replaced with `%2F`.

name: Get an environment secret
description: Retrieves metadata (name and timestamps) for a single secret in a GitHub Actions environment. Note: The actual secret value is never returned by this API for security reasons.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: Name of the secret to retrieve.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: Name of the environment (must be URL-encoded if it contains special characters).

name: Get an environment variable
description: Retrieves a specific environment variable from a GitHub Actions environment by repository owner, repository name, environment name, and variable name.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the specific environment variable to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub Actions environment. If the name contains special characters (e.g., slashes `/`), it must be URL-encoded (e.g., `staging/feature` becomes `staging%2Ffeature`).

name: Get an issue
description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific issue in a repository using the owner, repository name, and issue number.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository (without `.git` extension). Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: The identifying number of the issue.

name: Get an issue comment
description: Action to get an issue comment.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the issue comment.

name: Get an issue event
description: Fetches details of a specific issue event for a given repository and event ID.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: event_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier for the issue event.

name: Get an organization
description: Get an organization

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Get an organization public key
description: Action for `GetAnOrganizationPublicKey`.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Organization name (case-insensitive).

name: Get an organization role
description: Retrieves a specific GitHub organization role by its ID.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: role_id
inputs.description: The unique numerical identifier of the role within the organization.

name: Get an organization secret
description: Gets an organization secret's metadata (e.g., name, creation/update dates, visibility), but not its encrypted value.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: Name of the secret.

name: Get an organization variable
description: Retrieves details (name, value, visibility, timestamps) of a specific, existing variable for an existing GitHub organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the organization variable.

name: Get an organization webhook
description: Retrieves the full configuration, including subscribed events and delivery settings, for an existing organization webhook.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the webhook. You can find this value in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

name: Get a package for an organization
description: Retrieves a specific package (by type and name) from an organization, if both the package and organization exist.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.description: The unique name of the package.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.description: Type of the package. GitHub Gradle packages use `maven` type. Images from `ghcr.io` (Container registry) use `container` type. Use `docker` for images from `docker.pkg.github.com` (Docker registry), even if migrated to the Container registry.

name: Get a package for a user
description: Retrieves metadata for a specific package owned by a GitHub user, using package type, name, and username as identifiers.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: GitHub username of the package owner.

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.description: Unique name of the package on GitHub Packages.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.description: Specifies the package's type. Note: Gradle packages use 'maven'; ghcr.io images use 'container'; 'docker' can find images migrated from docker.pkg.github.com to the Container registry.

name: Get a package for the authenticated user
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific package owned by the authenticated user.

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.description: The unique name of the package as it appears in the registry.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.description: Type of the package. 'maven' for GitHub's Gradle registry; 'container' for Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (`ghcr.io`); 'docker' for images from GitHub's Docker registry (`docker.pkg.github.com`), including those migrated to the Container registry.

name: Get a package version for an organization
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific version of a package within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.description: Unique package name.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.description: Package type. `maven` for GitHub Gradle registry. `container` for GitHub Container registry (`ghcr.io`) images. `docker` for images from GitHub Docker registry (`docker.pkg.github.com`), including migrated ones.

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the package version.

name: Get a package version for a user
description: Retrieves a specific public package version associated with a GitHub user.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username of the account that owns the package.

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.description: The name of the package.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.description: The type of package. 'maven' is used for Gradle packages. 'container' is for images in GitHub's Container registry (ghcr.io). 'docker' can be used for images previously in GitHub's Docker registry (docker.pkg.github.com).

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.description: The unique numeric identifier of the specific package version. Obtain this ID by first listing package versions using the 'list_package_versions_for_a_package_owned_by_a_user' action.

name: Get authenticated user package version
description: Retrieves detailed information for an existing specific package version associated with the authenticated user, identified by its type, name, and version ID.

inputs.name: package_name
inputs.description: The name of the package, which can be case-sensitive depending on the package type.

inputs.name: package_type
inputs.description: The package type. Note: GitHub's Gradle registry packages use 'maven'; ghcr.io images use 'container'; 'docker' can refer to images from docker.pkg.github.com.

inputs.name: package_version_id
inputs.description: The unique numerical identifier (ID) of the specific package version.

name: Get a project
description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub project (V2) using its project number and owner. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 REST API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization that owns the project. If provided, this takes precedence over username.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username who owns the project. If not provided, defaults to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's sequential number (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This is displayed in the project URL as https://github.com/users/{username}/projects/{project_number}.

name: Get a pull request
description: Retrieves a specific pull request from a GitHub repository using its owner, repository name, and pull request number.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the .git extension. This value is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This value is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The number that identifies the specific pull request.

name: Get a reference
description: Retrieves a specific Git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a GitHub repository. The reference must exist in the repository; use 'list_matching_references' first if unsure what references are available.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The Git reference to retrieve. Must be formatted as `heads/<branch_name>` for branches, `tags/<tag_name>` for tags, or `pull/<number>/head` (or `pull/<number>/merge`) for pull requests. Simple branch names (e.g., `main`) are automatically converted to `heads/<name>`. The reference must exist in the repository; a 404 error is returned if not found. If unsure which references exist, use 'list_matching_references' or 'get_a_branch' first. Note: Commit SHAs are not supported by this endpoint; use commit-specific endpoints instead. Not all repositories use 'master'; many now use 'main' as the default branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get a release
description: Gets a specific release from a GitHub repository, provided the repository is accessible and the release exists.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.description: The unique numerical identifier for the release.

name: Get a release asset
description: Gets metadata for a specific release asset in a GitHub repository, including a `browser_download_url` for downloading the asset.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (GitHub username or organization name). This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: asset_id
inputs.description: The unique numeric identifier of the release asset.

name: Get a release by tag name
description: Gets a release from a GitHub repository by its tag name; the repository and a release with this tag must already exist.

inputs.name: tag
inputs.description: Tag identifying the release to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case sensitive).

name: Get a repository
description: Retrieves detailed information about an existing and accessible GitHub repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is case-insensitive.

name: Get a repository public key
description: Gets a repository's public key for encrypting secrets to be used in GitHub Actions, if the repository exists and is accessible.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

name: Get a repository readme
description: Fetches the README file (if it exists and is accessible) from a specified GitHub repository, returning its Base64-encoded content and metadata.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The specific git reference (branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) to retrieve the README from. If omitted, the repository's default branch is used.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, excluding the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The GitHub account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Get a repository readme for a directory
description: Retrieves the README file from a specified directory within a GitHub repository, optionally at a given commit, branch, or tag.

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description: Path to the directory containing the README file.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: Commit, branch, or tag name; defaults to the repository's default branch if not provided.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository (not case sensitive).

name: Get a repository ruleset
description: Retrieves a specific repository ruleset by its ID; if `includes_parents` is true, the search for this `ruleset_id` also extends to rulesets from parent organizations.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, excluding `.git` extension; case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (username or organization); case-insensitive.

inputs.name: ruleset_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the ruleset.

inputs.name: includes_parents
inputs.description: If true, also returns rulesets configured at higher levels (e.g., organization) that apply to this repository.

name: Get a repository rule suite
description: Gets detailed information for a specific repository rule suite by its ID, including its evaluation status and the results of its individual rules.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, excluding the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account or the name of the organization that owns the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: rule_suite_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the rule suite. To obtain this ID, you can list rule suites for the repository (e.g., via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets/rule-suites`) or organization (e.g., via `GET /orgs/{org}/rulesets/rule-suites`).

name: Get a repository secret
description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for an existing repository secret, excluding its encrypted value.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the secret to retrieve.

name: Get a repository subscription
description: Gets the authenticated user's subscription details for a repository, indicating if they receive notifications.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.

name: Get a repository variable
description: Gets a specific GitHub Actions variable by name from an accessible repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub Actions variable to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization that owns the repository.

name: Get a repository webhook
description: Returns the configuration of an existing webhook for a given repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a user or organization). The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: hook_id

name: Get a review comment for a pull request

name: List repository files and directories
description: Browse and list all files/directories in a GitHub repository. Efficiently retrieves the complete repository structure in a single API call when used with recursive mode. Perfect for analyzing codebase structure, finding specific files, or getting an overview of repository contents without cloning.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name without owner or .git extension (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, repo is 'kubernetes').

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Repository owner username or organization name (e.g., for github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, owner is 'kubernetes').

inputs.name: tree_sha
inputs.description: Branch name (e.g., 'main', 'master'), tag name (e.g., 'v1.0.0'), commit SHA, or tree SHA. Directory paths are NOT supported - use a branch/tag to get the full tree, then extract the directory's SHA from results.

inputs.name: recursive
inputs.description: Set to True (or '1'/'true') to fetch the ENTIRE repository file tree recursively (all files and directories in one call). Set to False or leave empty to fetch only the top-level files/folders. For browsing or analyzing the full repository structure, use recursive=True.

inputs.name: basehead
inputs.description: A string specifying the base and head Git revisions to compare, in the format `BASE_REF...HEAD_REF`. `BASE_REF` and `HEAD_REF` can be commit SHAs, branch names, or tags. For example, `main...develop` or `v1.0.0...v1.1.0`.

inputs.name: ghsa_id

name: Fetch GraphQL Node
description: Tool to fetch any GitHub object by its global node ID. Use when you have a node_id and need to retrieve the corresponding object details.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the object to fetch. This is the GraphQL global identifier (node_id) returned by GitHub's API, not the numeric ID.

inputs.name: fragment
inputs.description: Optional GraphQL fragment to specify which fields to retrieve from the returned node. Use GraphQL inline fragment syntax (e.g., '... on User { login name }'). If omitted, only the 'id' field will be returned.

name: Get GraphQL rate limit
description: Tool to retrieve the authenticated client's GitHub GraphQL API rate limit information. Use when you need to check remaining quota, current usage, or reset time for GraphQL requests.

inputs.name: dry_run
inputs.description: If true, calculate the cost for the query without evaluating it. Use this to estimate query costs without consuming actual quota.

name: Get information about a sarif upload
description: Retrieves detailed information, including processing status and results URL, about a specific SARIF (Static Analysis Results Interchange Format) upload for a repository, uniquely identified by its sarif_id.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account that owns the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: sarif_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the SARIF upload, returned when a SARIF file is successfully uploaded.

name: Get interaction restrictions for an organization
description: Retrieves interaction restrictions for an organization, showing which GitHub user types can interact with its public repositories and when restrictions expire; returns an empty response if no restrictions are set.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the organization. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Get interaction restrictions for a repository
description: Retrieves active interaction restrictions for a repository, detailing which user groups are limited from activities like commenting or creating pull requests, and when these restrictions expire.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is case-insensitive.

name: Get interaction restrictions for public repositories
description: Retrieves currently active interaction restrictions for the authenticated user's public repositories.

name: Get a label
description: Retrieves a specific label by its name from a specified GitHub repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the label to retrieve. GitHub's API treats label names as case-insensitive for this operation.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., a username or organization name). The name is not case-sensitive.

name: Get latest pages build
description: Retrieves information about the most recent GitHub Pages build for a repository, which must exist, be accessible, have GitHub Pages enabled, and have at least one prior build.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account or the name of the organization that owns the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

name: Get a license
description: Call this action to retrieve comprehensive details for a specific software license recognized by GitHub, using its unique license key.

inputs.name: license
inputs.description: The unique identifier (key) of the license. This is typically the SPDX license identifier (e.g., 'mit', 'apache-2.0').

name: Get License (GraphQL)
description: Tool to look up an open source license by its SPDX ID using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to fetch license details for compliance tracking or repository setup.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: The license's downcased SPDX ID. Common keys include 'mit', 'apache-2.0', 'gpl-3.0', 'bsd-3-clause', 'lgpl-3.0', 'mpl-2.0', 'agpl-3.0', 'unlicense', 'isc', 'bsd-2-clause'.

name: Get octocat
description: Fetches an ASCII art representation of GitHub's Octocat, suitable for text-based displays.

inputs.name: s
inputs.description: Custom message for Octocat's speech bubble. If omitted or empty, a random 'Zen of GitHub' message will be displayed instead.

name: Customize OIDC subject claim template
description: Retrieves the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim customization template for a repository, which defines the `sub` claim structure in OIDC tokens for GitHub Actions workflows; returns the default configuration if no customization is applied.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get allowed actions and workflows for an org
description: Gets the list of allowed actions and reusable workflows for an organization when the 'allowed_actions' policy is set to 'selected'.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The unique identifier or name of the organization. This field is not case sensitive.

name: Get organization audit log
description: Tool to retrieve an organization's audit log events for security and compliance investigations. Use when tracking organizational changes, security audits, or compliance monitoring. Requires organization owner permissions and read:audit_log scope.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: A cursor for pagination. Returns events after this cursor. Use the cursor from the response to fetch the next page.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: The order of audit log events. Can be 'asc' (ascending, oldest first) or 'desc' (descending, newest first). Defaults to 'desc'.

inputs.name: before
inputs.description: A cursor for pagination. Returns events before this cursor. Use the cursor from the response to fetch the previous page.

inputs.name: phrase
inputs.description: A search phrase to filter audit log events. For example, 'action:org' for organization-related events, or 'actor:octocat' for events by a specific user.

inputs.name: include
inputs.description: The event types to include. Can be 'web' (web interface events), 'git' (Git events), or 'all'. Defaults to 'web'.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of results per page (max 100). Defaults to 30.

name: Get a custom property for an organization
description: Retrieves the definition (schema) of a specific, existing custom property for an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Organization's login name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: custom_property_name
inputs.description: Name of the custom property to retrieve (case-sensitive).

name: Get organization project item
description: Tool to get an item from an organization-owned GitHub Projects V2 project. Use when you need to retrieve details about a specific project item including its content, fields, timestamps, and creator information.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: Limit results to specific fields, by their IDs. If not specified, the title field will be returned. Format: fields[]=123&fields[]=456 or fields=123,456,789

inputs.name: item_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the project item.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's number.

name: Get organization public key for Codespaces secrets
description: Retrieves an organization's public key, which must be used to encrypt secret values before they are configured for Codespaces.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Fetch Public Key For Secret Encryption
description: Retrieves the public key for an existing GitHub organization, required for encrypting Dependabot secrets. Requires admin:org scope.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization. Requires admin:org scope (must be an organization admin). This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get org dev environment secret safely
description: Retrieves metadata for a specific secret available to an organization's GitHub Codespaces without exposing its encrypted value.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (login). The name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the development environment secret to retrieve.

name: Get custom OIDC subject claim template
description: Retrieves the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim customization template for a GitHub organization, which defines how the `sub` claim in OIDC tokens for workflows is constructed.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the GitHub organization (case-insensitive). This is the organization's login/username, not a numeric ID.

name: Get a dns health check for github pages
description: Retrieves the DNS health check status (e.g., CNAME/A records, HTTPS) for a GitHub Pages site; the check may be pending (HTTP 202) on initial calls or after site changes.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case sensitive.

name: Get a GitHub Pages site
description: Retrieves information for a GitHub Pages site, which must be enabled for the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get page views
description: Retrieves page view statistics for a repository over the last 14 days, including total views, unique visitors, and a daily or weekly breakdown.

inputs.name: per
inputs.description: The time unit for which to aggregate page views.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is case-insensitive.

name: Get pending deployments for a workflow run
description: Retrieves pending deployment environments for a specific workflow run that are awaiting approval due to protection rules.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (user or organization); not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: Unique numerical identifier of the workflow run.

name: Get project permission for a user
description: Retrieves permission information for a GitHub project (classic) collaborator. Returns the permission level and full user object for a collaborator on a GitHub project (classic). The permission returned can be admin, write, read, or none.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username of the project owner or the user whose permission is being checked.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier (number) of the project.

name: Get public key for secret encryption
description: Retrieves a repository's public key for encrypting GitHub Codespaces secrets; requires `repo` scope or equivalent read access to codespaces secrets for private repositories.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

name: Get public key for the authenticated user
description: Retrieves the authenticated user's public key for encrypting GitHub Codespaces secrets. The returned key must be used with libsodium sealed box encryption before creating or updating secrets via the API.

name: Get pull request review protection
description: Retrieves the pull request review protection settings for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, if such protection is configured.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive. For example, in 'octocat/Hello-World', 'Hello-World' is the repository name.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive. For example, in 'octocat/Hello-World', 'octocat' is the owner.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Wildcard characters are not allowed. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GitHub GraphQL API.

name: Get rate limit status for the authenticated user
description: Retrieves the authenticated user's current GitHub API rate limit status, including usage and limits across different resource categories.

name: Get Raw Repository Content
description: Tool to fetch raw file content from a GitHub repository. Returns raw bytes in whatever format the file uses (JSON, CSV, binary, etc.) — parsing and validation are the caller's responsibility. Ensure owner, repo, ref, and path are consistent when combining with other GitHub tools to avoid mismatched-ref errors.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The branch name, tag name, or commit SHA to fetch the file from. Defaults to the repository's default branch (usually 'main' or 'master'). Recommended to pass explicitly — omitting it fetches from the default branch, which may not match the ref used in other tool calls and can yield unexpected or missing content.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: The file path within the repository, relative to the repository root. Use forward slashes for directory separators (e.g., 'src/utils/helper.js'). Do not include leading slash. Do NOT pass a full GitHub URL - only the relative path within the repo. Must exactly match the file path as it exists in the repository — use GITHUB_GET_A_TREE to discover valid paths rather than guessing directory structure.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The repository name (not the full URL). This is the repository name shown in the repository URL (e.g., for github.com/torvalds/linux, the repo is 'linux'). Do not include '.git' suffix. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. This is the account name shown in the repository URL (e.g., for github.com/torvalds/linux, the owner is 'torvalds'). Case-insensitive.

name: Get allowed actions and workflows for a repository
description: Gets the settings for allowed actions and reusable workflows in a repository. Requires the repository's `allowed_actions` policy to be set to `selected` (returns 409 if set to `all` or `local_only`).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get repository development environment secret
description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for a specific, existing development environment secret (Codespaces secret) in a repository, without exposing its value.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: secret_name
inputs.description: The name of the development environment secret to retrieve.

name: Get repository attestations
description: Tool to retrieve attestations by subject digest from a GitHub repository. Use when you need to verify or check attestations for a specific artifact identified by its SHA256 digest.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API".

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: before
inputs.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API".

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API".

inputs.name: predicate_type
inputs.description: Optional filter for fetching attestations with a given predicate type. This option accepts `provenance`, `sbom`, `release`, or freeform text for custom predicate types.

inputs.name: subject_digest
inputs.description: The parameter should be set to the attestation's subject's SHA256 digest, in the form `sha256:HEX_DIGEST`.

name: Get repository clones
description: Retrieves the total number of clones and a breakdown of clone activity (daily or weekly) for a specified repository over the preceding 14 days.

inputs.name: per
inputs.description: Specifies the time frame for aggregating clone data: `day` for daily clone counts, or `week` for weekly clone counts (a week starts on Monday).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the '.git' extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get repository content
description: Retrieves a file's Base64 encoded content or lists a directory's contents from a GitHub repository.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The name of the commit, branch, or tag to read from. Use this parameter to specify which branch (e.g., 'feat/godlocal-dev', 'main', 'develop'), tag (e.g., 'v1.2.0'), or commit SHA you want to access. If not provided, the repository's default branch will be used. Note: Branch names should be passed here, NOT in the path parameter.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Required. The path to the content in the repository. This can be a file or a directory path. Use an empty string to retrieve the contents of the repository's root directory. Leading slashes are automatically stripped (e.g., '/src/main.js' becomes 'src/main.js'). CRITICAL: Do NOT pass branch names here (e.g., 'feat/godlocal-dev', 'main', 'develop'). Branch names belong in the `ref` parameter, not the `path` parameter. The path should be the file or directory path within the repository (e.g., 'src/main.js', 'README.md'). Do NOT include the branch or ref name as a prefix in the path. For example, use 'src/main.js' not 'main/src/main.js' - the branch is specified separately via the ref parameter. File paths must include the file extension (e.g., 'README.md', not 'README'). For common files like README, LICENSE, or CHANGELOG without extensions, the action will automatically try common extensions (.md, .txt, .rst). Paths are case-sensitive and must match the exact casing in the repository (e.g., 'Docker' is different from 'docker'). If you receive a 404 'Not Found' error, verify the exact path by first listing the parent directory contents.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Required. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Required. The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive. Can also accept full repository path in format 'owner/repo' - the repo part will be extracted automatically.

name: Get Repository via GraphQL
description: Tool to lookup a repository by owner and name using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need comprehensive repository information.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the repository.

inputs.name: owner

inputs.name: followRenames
inputs.description: Follow repository renames. If true, the query will follow repository renames to find the current repository.

name: Get Repository Owner

inputs.name: login

inputs.name: owner

name: Get repository secret securely
description: Retrieves metadata for an existing Dependabot secret in a repository, without exposing its encrypted value.

inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

description: Tool to retrieve a repository security advisory using its GHSA identifier. Use when you need detailed information about a specific security vulnerability.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: ghsa_id

inputs.name: hook_id

inputs.name: delivery_id

description: Retrieves all active rules for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, excluding rules in 'evaluate' or 'disabled' status.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The GitHub account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Wildcard characters are not supported for this parameter; to use wildcards, refer to the GitHub GraphQL API.

inputs.name: per_page

inputs.name: org

inputs.name: secret_name

name: Get status checks protection
description: Retrieves the status check protection settings for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, if status check protection is enabled for it.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: The page number for the results to retrieve. See "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)" for more information.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Get the hourly commit count for each day

name: Get the latest release

name: Get the license for a repository

inputs.description: The specific Git reference (branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) to retrieve the license from. For a branch, it can be formatted as `refs/heads/<branch_name>` or simply `<branch_name>`. To reference a pull request's merge commit, use `refs/pull/<pull_number>/merge`. If omitted, the license from the repository's default branch is used.

inputs.description: The username of the account or the name of the organization that owns the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id

name: Get the status of a GitHub Pages deployment
description: Retrieves the status of a specific GitHub Pages deployment for a repository, which must have GitHub Pages enabled.

inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: repo

name: Get all commonly used licenses

inputs.name: per_page

name: Get all custom property values for a repository
description: Gets all custom property values for a repository. Custom properties are defined at the organization level, so this endpoint works with organization-owned repositories. Returns an empty array if no custom properties are set.

inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch (default 1).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (max 100, default 30).

inputs.name: repository_query
inputs.description: Optional query string to filter repositories. Uses GitHub's search syntax with keywords and qualifiers like 'is:public', 'topic:security', 'language:python', 'archived:false'. Leave empty to list all repositories.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results to return per page (max 100).

inputs.name: per_page

name: List notifications for authenticated user
description: Tool to list notification threads for the authenticated user with efficient polling support. Use when you need to fetch the user's GitHub notification inbox with filters like unread/participating/since. Returns notification threads plus polling headers (Last-Modified, ETag, X-Poll-Interval) for efficient polling. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a Personal Access Token (classic) with 'notifications' or 'repo' scope. It does NOT work with GitHub App tokens or fine-grained personal access tokens. If the token type is incompatible, a clear error message will be returned.

inputs.name: all
inputs.description: If true, show notifications marked as read. Default shows only unread notifications.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: since
inputs.description: Only show notifications updated after the given ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

inputs.name: before
inputs.description: Only show notifications updated before the given ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 50).

inputs.name: participating
inputs.description: If true, only shows notifications in which the user is directly participating or mentioned.

inputs.description: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.description: Field to sort results by.

inputs.description: Only issues updated at or after this time are returned. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

inputs.description: Filters issues by their state.

name: List org resource access tokens
description: Lists approved fine-grained personal access tokens (PATs) with access to resources in a GitHub organization, optionally filtering by owner, repository, permissions, or last usage time. AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (read). Standard personal access tokens CANNOT use this endpoint. The GitHub App must also have access to the organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: Name of the GitHub organization (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch (starts from 1).

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Property to sort results by. Currently only 'created_at' is supported.

inputs.name: token_id
inputs.description: Filter by a list of token IDs (max 50 items). Use this to look up specific tokens.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: Sort direction: 'asc' for ascending or 'desc' for descending (default).

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Filter by permission string in format 'scope:level' where scope is the permission area (e.g., 'issues', 'actions', 'contents') and level is 'read' or 'write'. Common examples: 'issues:read', 'issues:write', 'actions:read', 'actions:write', 'contents:read', 'contents:write', 'metadata:read', 'pull_requests:write'.

inputs.name: repository
inputs.description: Filter by repository name.

inputs.name: last_used_after
inputs.description: Filter for tokens last used after this ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

inputs.name: last_used_before
inputs.description: Filter for tokens last used before this ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).

name: List org resources with personal tokens
description: Lists requests from organization members to access organization resources with fine-grained personal access tokens (pending, approved, or denied). Note: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'organization_personal_access_token_requests:read' permissions; it cannot be used with personal access tokens.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The GitHub organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Filter by a list of token owner GitHub usernames.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: token_id
inputs.description: Filter by a list of token IDs (max 50 items). Use this to look up specific token requests.

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: Sort direction: 'asc' for ascending or 'desc' for descending.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: Filters requests by the specific permission granted to the token.

inputs.name: repository
inputs.description: Filter by repository name for tokens that access this repository.

inputs.name: last_used_after
inputs.description: Filters for tokens last used after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

inputs.name: last_used_before
inputs.description: Filters for tokens last used before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of results to return per page. The maximum value is 100. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

name: List pending team invitations
description: Lists all pending membership invitations for a specified team within an organization. The authenticated user must be an organization member with the read:org scope. The response includes invitation details such as the invitee's login/email, the role they're being invited to, and who sent the invitation. The 'role' field indicates the type of invitation: - 'direct_member': Regular organization member - 'admin': Organization administrator - 'billing_manager': Billing manager role - 'hiring_manager': Hiring manager role - 'reinstate': Reinstating a previous member Note: If the invitee is not a GitHub member, the 'login' field will be null and only the 'email' field will be populated.

name: List project collaborators (classic)
description: Lists collaborators for a GitHub Project (classic) by project ID. IMPORTANT: This action uses the Projects (classic) REST API which was sunset on April 1, 2025 for GitHub.com. The API may still be available on GitHub Enterprise Server versions before 3.17. For GitHub.com users: GitHub Projects (classic) has been replaced by the new Projects experience. The new Projects does not have a REST API for listing collaborators - use the GitHub web interface to manage project collaborators instead. You must be an organization owner or a project admin to list collaborators.

inputs.name: page

name: List project columns
description: Lists all of a GitHub project's columns (e.g., 'To Do', 'In Progress'); project_id must identify a valid, accessible project. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects (classic) REST API. inputs.description: Cursor for pagination to fetch results after this cursor.
