name: List workflow runs for a repository
description: Lists workflow runs for a repository, allowing filtering by actor, branch, event, status, creation date, check suite ID, or head SHA; the repository must exist and be accessible.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of the results to fetch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: actor
inputs.description: Filter by the actor's login (e.g., the user who triggered the `push`).

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: Filter by the event that triggered the workflow (e.g., `push`, `pull_request`).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Filter by branch name (e.g., `main` or the branch that triggered the `push`).

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Filter by `status` or `conclusion` (e.g., `success`, `in_progress`). Note: Only GitHub Actions can set `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested`.

inputs.name: created
inputs.description: Filter by creation date or date-time range (ISO8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). Supports operators like `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, and `..` for ranges.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.description: Filter to include only runs associated with the specified `head_sha` (commit SHA at the head of the branch).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: check_suite_id
inputs.description: Filter by a specific `check_suite_id`.

inputs.name: exclude_pull_requests
inputs.description: Set to `true` to exclude workflow runs triggered by pull request events. Omit or leave as null to include all runs.

name: List workflow runs for a workflow
description: Lists runs for a specified, existing workflow (identified by ID or filename like `main.yml`) in a GitHub repository, with filtering options.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of the results to retrieve.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: actor
inputs.description: Filter by the GitHub username of the actor who initiated the run.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: Filter by the event that triggered the run (e.g., `push`, `pull_request`). For more event types, see GitHub's documentation on "Events that trigger workflows".

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Filter by branch name, typically for `push` events.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Filter by status or conclusion (e.g., `success`, `in_progress`). Note: `waiting`, `pending`, or `requested` statuses are set exclusively by GitHub Actions.

inputs.name: created
inputs.description: Filter by creation date/time range. For syntax, refer to GitHub's "Understanding the search syntax" guide for dates.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.description: Filter by the head commit's SHA.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (max 100).

inputs.name: workflow_id
inputs.description: The ID of the workflow (integer) or the workflow file name (string, e.g., `main.yml` or `ci.yaml`).

inputs.name: check_suite_id
inputs.description: Filter by `check_suite_id` (identifier for a collection of check runs).

inputs.name: exclude_pull_requests
inputs.description: If true, exclude workflow runs triggered by pull requests.

name: Lookup Enterprise Administrator Invitation

inputs.name: enterprise_slug
inputs.description: The slug of the enterprise the user was invited to join. This is the URL-friendly identifier for the enterprise.

name: Lookup GitHub Resource
description: Tool to lookup a GitHub resource by its URL. Use when you need to identify or retrieve information about a GitHub resource from its URL.

description: Tool to lookup GitHub nodes by their global node IDs. Use when you need to retrieve multiple objects by their IDs in a single batch query.

inputs.description: A list of GitHub global node IDs to lookup. Each ID must be a valid base64-encoded global node ID (e.g., 'MDQ6VXNlcjU4MzIzMQ==').

name: Look Up Topic by Name
description: Tool to look up a GitHub topic by name using GraphQL. Use when you need to find information about a specific GitHub topic including its popularity (stargazer count) and related topics.

inputs.description: The topic's name to look up (e.g., 'python', 'javascript', 'machine-learning').

inputs.name: related_topics_count
inputs.description: The number of related topics to fetch (between 1 and 10). Only used if include_related_topics is true.

inputs.name: include_related_topics
inputs.description: Whether to include related topics in the response. If true, up to 3 related topics will be returned.

name: Merge a branch
description: Merges a head branch or commit SHA into a base branch in a repository; fails if there are merge conflicts requiring manual resolution.

inputs.name: base
inputs.description: The name of the branch to merge the `head` branch into.

inputs.name: head
inputs.description: Source branch name or full commit SHA to merge into the `base` branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: commit_message
inputs.description: Commit message for the merge. If omitted, GitHub uses a default message.

name: Merge a pull request
description: Merges an open and mergeable pull request in a repository. A 405 error can occur for multiple reasons: (1) The PR is still in draft mode - convert it to ready-for-review first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST. (2) Branch protection rules are not satisfied - check for required approving reviews, status checks, or other repository rules that must be met before merging. (3) The base branch has been modified since the PR was last synced - update the PR branch first using GITHUB_UPDATE_A_PULL_REQUEST_BRANCH.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: SHA of the pull request's head commit. Must match for merge to succeed, ensuring PR hasn't changed since review.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Repository owner's username. Not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the pull request. The PR must be mergeable: not a draft, and all branch protection rules (required reviews, status checks, etc.) must be satisfied.

inputs.name: commit_title
inputs.description: Title for the merge commit message.
examples: Fixing a typo in the README file

inputs.name: merge_method
inputs.description: Merge strategy: 'merge', 'squash', or 'rebase'. Defaults to repository's setting if unspecified.

inputs.name: commit_message
inputs.description: Additional details for the merge commit message.
examples: This change updates the project description to be more accurate.

name: Ping a repository webhook
description: Pings an existing webhook on a repository to test its configuration and reachability by GitHub.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the hook. You can find this value in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

name: Publish Sponsors Tier (GraphQL)

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

inputs.name: delivery_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of a specific past delivery for the webhook (obtainable by listing deliveries for that webhook).

inputs.name: owner

description: Removes all labels from a specified issue in a GitHub repository; this operation is idempotent.

inputs.name: issue_number

name: Remove a repository collaborator
description: Removes a collaborator from a specified GitHub repository, provided the repository exists and the user is an existing collaborator.

inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username of the collaborator to remove.

name: Remove status check contexts
description: Removes specified status check contexts from a protected branch's required status checks. This action removes the specified status check contexts from the existing list of required status checks for a protected branch. The branch must already have branch protection enabled with status checks configured. Note: The 'contexts' parameter is deprecated by GitHub in favor of 'checks' array. For new implementations, consider using update_status_check_protection with the 'checks' parameter instead.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch. Wildcard characters are not allowed. To use wildcard characters in branch names, refer to the GraphQL API documentation.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.description: The status check contexts to remove from the protected branch. These are the names of CI/CD jobs or other checks that should no longer be required before merging. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings. Examples: 'ci/test', 'linter', 'continuous-integration/travis-ci'.

name: Remove status check protection
description: Removes status check protection from a branch, disabling required status checks for merging pull requests; will only have an effect if status check protection is currently configured on the branch.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner (username or organization) of the repository; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Name of the branch; wildcard characters are not allowed.

name: Render Markdown (Raw Mode)
description: Tool to render raw Markdown text to HTML using GitHub's markdown API. Use when you need to convert plain Markdown content to HTML without GitHub-specific features like issue references or @mentions.

inputs.name: markdown_content
inputs.description: The raw Markdown text to render as HTML. This can include any valid Markdown syntax like headers, lists, links, bold/italic text, code blocks, etc.
examples:
# Hello World

This is a **test** markdown document with:
- List item 1
- List item 2

[Link](https://github.com)

name: Replace all repository topics
description: Replaces all topics of a repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is case-insensitive.

inputs.name: names
inputs.description: A list of topic names to replace the current set of topics for the repository. Provide one or more topics to set them. To remove all topics, pass an empty list (`[]`). Note: Topic names cannot contain uppercase letters and are limited to 25 characters.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is case-insensitive.

name: Add reviewers to a specific pull request
description: Adds reviewers to a specific pull request identified by owner, repo, and pull_number. This is NOT a search action - it operates on an already-known PR number. Requires `owner`, `repo`, `pull_number`, and at least one of `reviewers` or `team_reviewers`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (username or organization name); not case sensitive.

inputs.name: reviewers
inputs.description: GitHub user `login` strings (usernames) to request reviews from. At least one of `reviewers` or `team_reviewers` must be provided.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: Number identifying the pull request.

inputs.name: team_reviewers
inputs.description: GitHub team `slug` strings (e.g., 'justice-league') to request reviews from. Slugs are typically lowercase and use hyphens for spaces. At least one of `reviewers` or `team_reviewers` must be provided.

name: Rerun a workflow
description: Re-runs a specific GitHub Actions workflow run identified by its owner, repository, and run ID, optionally enabling debug logging.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository (without the `.git` extension) where the workflow run exists (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository where the workflow run exists (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the workflow run to re-execute.

inputs.name: enable_debug_logging
inputs.description: Set to `true` to enable diagnostic logging for the re-run, helpful for troubleshooting.

name: Rerun failed jobs from a workflow run
description: Re-runs all failed jobs and their dependent jobs from a specified workflow run if the run contains previously failed jobs.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (username or organization name); not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the workflow run.

inputs.name: enable_debug_logging
inputs.description: Set to `true` to enable extensive debug logging for the re-run, providing additional logs to aid in diagnosing issues.

name: Retire Sponsors Tier
description: Tool to retire a published payment tier from your GitHub Sponsors profile using GraphQL. Use when you need to prevent a tier from being used for new sponsorships. Existing sponsors at this tier remain unaffected.

inputs.name: tier_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the sponsors tier to retire (e.g., 'ST_kwDOCNqc1s4ACOVb'). Once retired, this tier cannot be used to start new sponsorships, but existing sponsors at this tier are unaffected.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations and track request origin.

name: Run GraphQL Query
description: Tool to run an arbitrary GitHub GraphQL v4 query or mutation. Use when fetching multiple datasets in one batch. Cost-based rate limit: ~5,000 points/hour; keep field selections narrow, avoid deep nesting, and include rateLimit in responses to monitor quota. Responses are nested under data.data (e.g., data.data.repository.vulnerabilityAlerts); always inspect the errors array even on HTTP 200, as partial failures embed there. Paginate by looping on pageInfo.hasNextPage and advancing with pageInfo.endCursor. Search results cap at ~1,000 items per query; split by label, date range, or repository for full coverage. Use ProjectV2 nodes, not Classic Projects (deprecated). Parallel mutations (e.g., addProjectV2ItemById) can trigger transient conflicts; use sequential execution.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: GraphQL query or mutation string. Must comply with GitHub's GraphQL v4 schema.

inputs.name: variables
inputs.description: JSON object mapping variable names to values for use in the query.

name: Run GraphQL Relay Query
description: Tool to run a GitHub GraphQL relay query. The relay field is a workaround for re-exposing the root query object. Use when you need to access root query fields through the relay context.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: GraphQL query string to execute within the relay context. Must comply with GitHub's GraphQL v4 schema. The relay field re-exposes the root Query object, allowing you to nest any valid root query fields.
examples: query { relay { repository(owner: "github", name: "docs") { name description } } }

inputs.name: variables
inputs.description: JSON object mapping variable names to values for use in the query. Optional - omit if the query has no variables.

name: Search code
description: Searches code file contents and paths on the default branch of GitHub repositories using a query string; searches only files under 384KB, returns max 1000 results by best match, and is optimized for precision.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Code search query, including keywords and qualifiers (e.g., `language:python`, `user:octocat`, `repo:owner/repo`). For full syntax and qualifiers, see GitHub docs: [Constructing a search query](https://docs.github.com/rest/search/search#constructing-a-search-query) and [code search qualifiers](https://docs.github.com/search-github/searching-on-github/searching-code).

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number for results. For pagination details, see GitHub docs: [Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api).

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: DEPRECATED. Valid: 'indexed' (sorts by last indexed time) or 'updated' (sorts by last updated time). If unspecified, results sorted by best match. Docs: [ranking search results](https://docs.github.com/rest/search/search#ranking-search-results).

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: DEPRECATED. Order ('asc' or 'desc') for results; effective only if `sort` is specified. Docs: [ranking search results](https://docs.github.com/rest/search/search#ranking-search-results).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Results per page (max 100). For pagination details, see GitHub docs: [Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api).

name: Search code (all pages)
description: Tool to search code across multiple pages using GitHub's code search API. Use when single-page searches may miss matches and you need a full or capped result set. GitHub caps results at ~1,000 total items regardless of pagination; results for broad queries may be silently truncated. Only the default branch is indexed, and very large files may be excluded — treat results as potentially partial. Rate limit: ~30 requests/minute; honor `Retry-After` on 403/429 responses.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: The search query string for code search. **IMPORTANT**: While GitHub's documentation mentions boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT) support, the REST API still uses older syntax that frequently rejects queries with operators or parentheses (ERROR_TYPE_QUERY_PARSING_FATAL). For reliable searches, avoid boolean operators and parentheses. Supports qualifiers: `language:`, `repo:`, `path:`, `extension:`, `user:`, `org:`, `in:file`. Multiple terms use implicit AND: `hello world` finds both. Quote exact phrases: `"exact phrase"`. For complex boolean logic, use multiple separate API calls or the GitHub web interface.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number to start retrieval (min 1).

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Sort field: only 'indexed' is valid (sort by last indexed time).

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Optional cap on total number of items to retrieve across pages (min 1).

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order for results: 'asc' or 'desc'. Only effective when `sort` is provided.

inputs.name: accept
inputs.description: Custom Accept header for media type (e.g., `application/vnd.github.text-match+json` to include text match metadata).

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (min 1, max 100).

name: Search commits
description: Finds commits on GitHub using a query string (q) supporting keywords and qualifiers, with options for sorting and pagination.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Search query with at least one text keyword plus optional qualifiers. GitHub requires actual search text (commit message keywords) - qualifiers alone are not allowed. Common qualifiers: 'repo:owner/name', 'author:username', 'committer:username', 'author-date:YYYY-MM-DD' or 'author-date:>YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD', 'committer-date:YYYY-MM-DD', 'merge:true/false', 'hash:HASH', 'parent:HASH', 'tree:HASH'. IMPORTANT: The 'hash:' qualifier requires a full 40-character hexadecimal commit hash (e.g., 'hash:e5bd3914e2e596debea16f433f57875b5b90bcd6'). Do NOT use branch names, tag names, or short refs with 'hash:' - use 'repo:owner/name' with other filters to search commits on a specific branch.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to retrieve.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Sorts results by 'author-date' or 'committer-date'. Use separately with the 'order' parameter (e.g., sort='committer-date' with order='desc'). Do not combine them into a single value. If unspecified, GitHub defaults to 'best match' ranking.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order: 'desc' (newest first) or 'asc' (oldest first). Use with the 'sort' parameter to control direction (e.g., sort='committer-date' with order='desc').

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

name: Search commits by author across repos
description: Searches commits across GitHub using the Search API (e.g., by author/email/org/date range) without per-repo scanning. Use when you need to find commits by author, committer email, organization, or date ranges across multiple repositories. This avoids the need to list commits per repository and prevents 409 errors on empty repos.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: GitHub search query for commits. IMPORTANT: Must include search text (keywords) in addition to qualifiers. Qualifier-only queries are not allowed by the GitHub API. Supports qualifiers like author:, committer-email:, org:, repo:, author-date:, committer-date:, merge:, hash:, parent:, tree:, is:public/private. Examples: 'fix author:octocat', 'bug repo:owner/repo', 'update author:octocat author-date:>=2025-01-01'. If only qualifiers are provided, a wildcard search term will be automatically added.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of results to fetch.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Sort options for commit search.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Order options for search results.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100).

inputs.name: composio_execution_message
inputs.description: Message about any automatic modifications made to the input

name: GitHub GraphQL Search
description: Tool to perform GitHub GraphQL searches across issues, pull requests, repositories, users, and discussions. Returns up to 1,000 results with cursor-based pagination. Use when you need structured search results with specific fields.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of resource to search. Determines the structure of returned results.

inputs.name: after
inputs.description: Cursor string obtained from pageInfo.endCursor in a previous response. Returns results after this cursor for pagination.

inputs.name: first
inputs.description: Number of results to return (1-100). Returns the first N elements from the result set. Use with 'after' parameter for pagination.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: The search query string using GitHub search syntax. Supports qualifiers like 'repo:', 'org:', 'user:', 'is:open', 'is:closed', 'label:', etc. For issues/PRs, use 'is:issue' or 'is:pr'. For repositories, search by name, description, or topics. Maximum 1,000 results can be retrieved across all pages.

name: Search issues and pull requests
description: Searches GitHub for issues and pull requests. Supports keywords, qualifiers (repo:, org:, user:, state:, label:, author:, assignee:, mentions:, etc.), and type filters (is:issue, is:pr). Type filters cannot be used alone - they must be combined with at least one keyword or other qualifier. All other qualifiers work independently. The @me shorthand (e.g., assignee:@me, mentions:@me) is automatically resolved to your username. Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) are supported but limited to a maximum of 5 operators total per query.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Search query using GitHub's search syntax. Supports keywords, qualifiers (repo:, org:, user:, state:, label:, author:, assignee:, mentions:, etc.), and type filters (is:issue, is:pr). IMPORTANT: Type filters (is:issue, is:pr) cannot be used alone - they must be combined with at least one keyword or other qualifier. All other qualifiers work independently (e.g., 'state:open', 'label:bug', 'assignee:octocat'). The @me shorthand (e.g., assignee:@me, mentions:@me, author:@me) can be used to reference the authenticated user. Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) can be used to combine terms (maximum 5 operators total).
examples: bug repo:octocat/Spoon-Knife is:issue is:open label:"help wanted"

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number for results.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Field for sorting results. GitHub defaults to 'best match' if unspecified.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Order for sorted results (ascending/descending). Effective only if `sort` is also provided.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of results per page.

inputs.name: response_detail
inputs.description: Level of detail in the response. 'minimal' (default) returns an optimized response for AI agents. 'full' returns the complete GitHub API response.

name: Search labels
description: Searches for labels within a GitHub repository by keywords in their names or descriptions.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: The search keywords or query to find labels. This endpoint does not accept qualifiers in the query. For more details on query construction, refer to GitHub's documentation on [Constructing a search query](https://docs.github.com/rest/search/search#constructing-a-search-query).

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: The page number of the results to retrieve, starting from 1. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Specifies the field to sort the search results by. Valid options are 'created' or 'updated'. If not provided, results are sorted by best match according to GitHub's default ranking: [best match](https://docs.github.com/rest/search/search#ranking-search-results).

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Determines the sorting order of results, either 'asc' (ascending) or 'desc' (descending). This parameter is only effective when `sort` is also specified. Defaults to 'desc'.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of search results to return per page. The maximum value is 100. For more information, see "[Using pagination in the REST API](https://docs.github.com/rest/using-the-rest-api/using-pagination-in-the-rest-api)."

inputs.name: repository_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the repository where labels will be searched.

name: Search repositories
description: Searches GitHub repositories using a flexible query (keywords, qualifiers) with sorting, ordering, and pagination. IMPORTANT: The 'q' parameter (search query) is REQUIRED and must always be provided.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: REQUIRED non-empty search query. Max 5 AND/OR/NOT operators. Qualifiers: `language:LANG`, `stars:N`, `stars:>N`, `forks:N`, `size:N` (KB), `topic:TOPIC`, `license:LICENSE`, `created:DATE`, `pushed:DATE`, `is:public|private`, `archived:true|false`, `in:name|description|readme`, `user:USERNAME`, `org:ORGNAME`. Must use real GitHub usernames/orgs (e.g., `user:octocat`), not placeholders. Queries with logical operators must include at least one plain keyword; `topic:solana OR topic:ethereum` fails, use `solana OR ethereum stars:>100` instead.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: The page number for pagination of search results. Defaults to 1. Used in conjunction with `per_page` to fetch subsequent sets of results. See GitHub API documentation for more on pagination.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Specifies the field to sort the search results by. Options include `stars`, `forks`, `help-wanted-issues`, `updated` (recency of update), or `created` (creation date). If not provided, results are sorted by GitHub's default 'best match' ranking.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Determines the order of search results: `desc` for descending or `asc` for ascending. This parameter is only effective when `sort` is also specified. Defaults to `desc`.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: The number of repository results to return per page. The maximum value is 100. Defaults to 30. See GitHub API documentation for more on pagination.

name: Search topics
description: Finds topics on GitHub using keywords and qualifiers with GitHub's search syntax, supporting pagination.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Search query string. Supports keywords, qualifiers, and GitHub's search syntax; see [Constructing a search query](https://docs.github.com/rest/search/search#constructing-a-search-query).

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number of the results to retrieve for pagination.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of topic results per page (maximum 100).

name: Search users
description: Searches for users on GitHub by criteria like username, email, location, followers, or repository associations, using a flexible query string `q`.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Search query string supporting keywords and qualifiers for user attributes (e.g., `username`, `location`, `followers`). See examples for syntax.

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Page number for results (1-indexed).

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description: Field to sort results by. If not specified, GitHub's 'best match' algorithm is used.

inputs.name: order
inputs.description: Sort order for results when `sort` is specified; ignored otherwise.

inputs.name: per_page
inputs.description: Number of search results per page. Maximum 100.

name: Set labels for an issue
description: Replaces all existing labels on a GitHub issue with a new set of labels.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: A list of label names to apply to the issue. This replaces all existing labels on the issue. Providing an empty list will remove all labels from the issue.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: The number that identifies the issue within the repository.

name: Set status check contexts
description: Replaces required status check contexts for a protected branch, requiring admin permissions; an empty `contexts` array removes all checks.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner (username or organization) of the repository; not case sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch for which to set status check contexts; cannot contain wildcard characters.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.description: Names of the status checks to require; an empty array removes all required status checks.

name: Set User Interaction Limit (GraphQL)
description: Tool to set interaction limits on a user's public repositories using GraphQL. Use when you need to temporarily restrict who can comment, open issues, or create pull requests. Limits apply to the authenticated user or a specified user.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: The level of interaction to limit. EXISTING_USERS limits to users with accounts older than 24 hours, CONTRIBUTORS_ONLY limits to prior contributors, COLLABORATORS_ONLY limits to collaborators only.

inputs.name: expiry
inputs.description: When the interaction limit expires.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.description: The global ID of the user to set interaction limits for. This is required and must be a valid GitHub user node ID.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.

name: Star a gist
description: Stars a GitHub gist identified by `gist_id`; this action is idempotent and returns a 204 No Content status upon success, even if the gist is already starred.

inputs.name: gist_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the gist. This ID is the string of characters found at the end of a gist's URL.

name: Star a specified repository (requires owner and repo)
description: Stars a specified repository (identified by owner and repo name) for the authenticated user; this action is idempotent and succeeds even if the repository is already starred. Requires explicit repository identification via owner and repo parameters.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Required. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Required. The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

name: Submit a review for a pull request
description: Finalizes a pending pull request review (identified by `review_id`) with a required `event` (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, COMMENT) and an optional `body`.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The main textual content of the review. Highly recommended for `COMMENT` events to provide context; can also supplement `APPROVE` or `REQUEST_CHANGES` actions.
examples: Consider refactoring this part for clarity.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The review action: `APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, or `COMMENT`. An invalid/omitted event will result in an HTTP 422 error and set the review state to `PENDING`, requiring a subsequent valid submission.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. Case-insensitive.

inputs.name: review_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the pending review to be submitted. Typically obtained when a review is first created or initiated.

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The number that uniquely identifies the pull request.

name: Sync a fork branch with the upstream repository
description: Synchronizes a branch in a forked GitHub repository with its upstream counterpart, assuming the repository is a fork, the branch exists, an upstream is configured, and the merge is conflict-free.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the forked repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name of the account that owns the forked repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch in the forked repository that should be updated to match the corresponding branch in the upstream repository.

name: Unfollow organization
description: Tool to unfollow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to stop following a specific organization on GitHub.

inputs.name: organizationId
inputs.description: The Node ID of the organization to unfollow (e.g., 'MDEyOk9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbjk5MTk='). This is the global GraphQL node ID, not the organization name or numeric ID.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This value will be returned in the response and can be used for request tracking or idempotency.

name: Unfollow user
description: Tool to unfollow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to stop following a specific user on GitHub.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The Node ID of the user to unfollow (e.g., 'MDQ6VXNlcjE='). This is the global GraphQL node ID, not the username or numeric user ID.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This value will be returned in the response and can be used for request tracking or idempotency.

name: Update a code scanning alert
description: Updates a specific code scanning alert in a GitHub repository, primarily to change its state (e.g., 'open' or 'dismissed').

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: The new state for the code scanning alert. Must be either 'open' or 'dismissed'. If 'dismissed', `dismissed_reason` is mandatory.

inputs.name: alert_number
inputs.description: The number that identifies an alert. Found in the alert's URL or the `number` field of the GET alerts API response.

inputs.name: dismissed_reason
inputs.description: The reason for dismissing or closing the alert. **Required if `state` is 'dismissed'.**

inputs.name: dismissed_comment
inputs.description: An optional comment to provide further context when dismissing an alert. Maximum 280 characters.
examples: This vulnerability is mitigated by an external control.
Accepted risk for this specific scenario.

name: Update a discussion
description: Updates the title and/or body of a specific team discussion within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The new body text for the discussion post. Supports markdown formatting. Only provide this if you want to change the body content.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The new title for the discussion post. Only provide this if you want to change the title.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The slug of the team name.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.description: The unique number that identifies the discussion within the team.

name: Update a discussion comment
description: Updates the body of a comment in a team's discussion within an organization.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The name of the organization. This is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The new body text for the discussion comment. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.
examples: I disagree with this point, let's discuss further.

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The slug of the team name. This is the team name converted to lowercase with spaces replaced by hyphens (e.g., 'justice-league').

inputs.name: comment_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the comment within the discussion.

inputs.name: discussion_number
inputs.description: The unique number identifying the discussion.

name: Update a label
description: Updates an existing label's name, color, or description within a specified repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Current name of the label to be updated; value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the .git extension; value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: color
inputs.description: Hexadecimal color code for the label, without the leading '#' (e.g., 'f29513').

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository; value is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.description: New name for the label (not case-sensitive). Emoji can be added using native characters or colon-style markup (e.g., ':strawberry:'). Refer to GitHub's emoji cheat sheet for a full list of codes.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Short description for the label (max 100 characters).

name: Update a milestone
description: Updates a milestone in a repository, identified by `owner`, `repo`, and `milestone_number`, by allowing modification of its `title`, `state`, `description`, or `due_on`; at least one of these four attributes must be provided to perform an update.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: The new state of the milestone. If not provided, the state will remain unchanged. Defaults to 'open'.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The new title for the milestone. If not provided, the title will remain unchanged.

inputs.name: due_on
inputs.description: The milestone due date in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`. If not provided, the due date will remain unchanged. To remove the due date, pass an empty string or `null`.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A new description for the milestone. If not provided, the description will remain unchanged.

inputs.name: milestone_number
inputs.description: The unique number that identifies the milestone.

name: Update an environment variable
description: Updates an existing environment variable's name and/or value in a specific GitHub repository environment; requires providing either a new name or a new value.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Current name of the environment variable to update.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: New value for the environment variable; if omitted, the value is not changed.

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.description: New name for the environment variable. If provided, the variable will be renamed to this value. If omitted, the name remains unchanged.

inputs.name: environment_name
inputs.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded (e.g., replace slashes `/` with `%2F`).

name: Update an issue
description: Updates an existing GitHub issue's title, body, state, milestone, labels, or assignees; `state_reason` is only processed if `state` also changes, and use `null` or `[]` to clear applicable fields.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: New issue body; send `null` to clear, omit to leave unchanged.
examples:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Do this
2. Do that
Observed behavior...

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: New issue state; omit to leave unchanged.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: New issue title; send `null` to clear, omit to leave unchanged.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: Replaces all existing labels. Send `[]` to remove all; omit to leave unchanged. Requires push access for changes.

inputs.name: assignee
inputs.description: DEPRECATED: Username to assign; use `assignees`. Send `null` to unassign, omit to leave unchanged.

inputs.name: assignees
inputs.description: Replaces all existing assignees. Send `[]` to remove all; omit to leave unchanged. Requires push access for changes.

inputs.name: milestone
inputs.description: Milestone ID (as string) or title. Send `null` to clear; omit to leave unchanged. Numeric ID recommended.

inputs.name: issue_number
inputs.description: Unique number identifying the issue.

inputs.name: state_reason
inputs.description: Reason for state change, considered only if `state` also changes. Valid reasons depend on the new `state`.

name: Update an issue comment
description: Updates an existing comment on an issue or pull request within a specified repository. You can only update comments that the authenticated user has permission to edit. The comment_id can be obtained from the list_issue_comments or get_an_issue_comment actions.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: New content for the issue comment (GitHub Flavored Markdown supported).
examples: This is the updated comment body. :tada:

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username of the account owning the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the issue comment to update.

name: Update a project
description: Updates an existing GitHub project (V2) attributes like title, description, visibility, and state. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed. At least one field to update must be provided.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization that owns the project. If provided, this takes precedence over username.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The new title for the project. If not provided, the title will not be changed.

inputs.name: closed
inputs.description: Sets whether the project is closed. `True` closes the project, `False` reopens it. If not provided, the state will not be changed.

inputs.name: public
inputs.description: Sets the visibility of the project. `True` makes it public (visible to everyone), `False` makes it private. If not provided, the visibility will not be changed.

inputs.name: readme
inputs.description: The project's README content (supports Markdown formatting). If not provided, the README will not be changed.
examples:
# Project README

This project tracks our Q3 roadmap.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The GitHub username who owns the project. If not provided, defaults to the authenticated user.

inputs.name: project_number
inputs.description: The project's sequential number (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This is displayed in the project URL as https://github.com/users/{username}/projects/{project_number}.

inputs.name: short_description
inputs.description: A short description for the project (displayed in project cards and listings). If not provided, the description will not be changed.

name: Update a pull request
description: Updates an existing pull request, allowing changes to attributes like title, body, state, base branch, and maintainer modification settings.

inputs.name: base
inputs.description: The name of the branch to which you want your changes pulled. This must be an existing branch in the current repository. Cannot update the base branch to point to another repository. If None, the base branch remains unchanged.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The new contents of the pull request. To remove the body, pass an empty string. If None, the body remains unchanged.
examples: This PR updates the README.md to include details about the new /users endpoint.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: The new state of the pull request. Can be 'open' or 'closed'. If None, the state remains unchanged.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The new title for the pull request. If None, the title remains unchanged.
examples: Update documentation for new API endpoint

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: The unique number that identifies the pull request within the repository.

inputs.name: maintainer_can_modify
inputs.description: Indicates whether [maintainers can modify](https://docs.github.com/articles/allowing-changes-to-a-pull-request-branch-created-from-a-fork/) the pull request. If None, this setting remains unchanged.

name: Update a pull request branch
description: Updates an open pull request's head branch by merging the latest changes from its base branch, if mergeable and repository merging is enabled; operates asynchronously.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: pull_number
inputs.description: Unique number identifying the pull request.

inputs.name: expected_head_sha
inputs.description: SHA of the expected head commit of the pull request's branch. If this SHA does not match the actual head SHA, the request fails (422 error), ensuring the branch was not updated unexpectedly. If omitted, the API uses the current head SHA of the branch.

name: Update a reference
description: Updates an existing Git reference (e.g., a branch or tag) to point to a specific commit SHA. By default, only allows fast-forward updates (where the new commit is a descendant of the current one). Set `force=true` to allow non-fast-forward updates that rewrite history. IMPORTANT: The reference must already exist - use 'Create a reference' action to create new refs.

inputs.name: ref
inputs.description: The name of the Git reference to update WITHOUT the 'refs/' prefix. For branches: use 'heads/<branch-name>' (e.g., 'heads/main', 'heads/feature-branch'). For tags: use 'tags/<tag-name>' (e.g., 'tags/v1.0.0'). Note: Branch references require 'heads/' (plural), not 'head/' (singular). Bare branch names will be automatically prefixed with 'heads/'. The reference must already exist - this endpoint only updates existing refs.

inputs.name: sha
inputs.description: The SHA1 commit hash to point this reference to. Must be exactly 40 hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f) representing a valid commit that exists in the repository.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, excluding `.git` extension (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: force
inputs.description: Controls whether to allow non-fast-forward updates. When `false` (default), the new commit must be a direct descendant of the current commit (fast-forward only) - this prevents accidental history rewriting. When `true`, allows moving the reference to any commit, even if it means rewriting history or moving backward. Set to `true` only when you need to force-push changes, reset a branch, or intentionally rewrite commit history.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository (case-insensitive).

name: Update a release
description: Updates an existing release in a GitHub repository, allowing modification of its attributes; if linking a discussion, the `discussion_category_name` must refer to an existing category in the repository.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Text describing the contents of the release. Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown.
examples: This release includes performance improvements and bug fixes related to user authentication.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The title of the release.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: draft
inputs.description: Set to `true` to mark the release as a draft, or `false` to publish it. Default: `false`.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: tag_name
inputs.description: The name of the tag. This can be an existing tag or a new tag name.

inputs.name: prerelease
inputs.description: Set to `true` to identify the release as a pre-release, or `false` for a full release. Default: `false`.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the release to be updated.

inputs.name: make_latest
inputs.description: Specifies whether this release should be set as the latest release for the repository. Accepts 'true', 'false', or 'legacy'. Drafts and pre-releases cannot be set as latest. Defaults to `true` for newly published releases. `legacy` specifies that the latest release should be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version.

inputs.name: target_commitish
inputs.description: Specifies the commitish value (e.g., branch name or commit SHA) that determines where the Git tag is created from. Can be any branch or commit SHA. This parameter is unused if the Git tag specified by `tag_name` already exists. Defaults to the repository's default branch if omitted.

inputs.name: discussion_category_name
inputs.description: If specified, a discussion of this category is created and linked to the release. The category must already exist in the repository. This parameter is ignored if a discussion is already linked to the release. For more information, see "[Managing categories for discussions in your repository](https://docs.github.com/discussions/managing-discussions-for-your-community/managing-categories-for-discussions-in-your-repository)."

name: Update a repository
description: Updates settings for an existing GitHub repository, such as name, description, visibility, merge strategies, and security configurations.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: New name for the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: ACTUAL name of an existing repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real repository that exists and is accessible - placeholder values like 'your-repo-name' will cause 404 errors.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: ACTUAL GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository (not case-sensitive). MUST be a real, existing account - placeholder values like 'your-username' will cause 404 errors.

inputs.name: private
inputs.description: Whether the repository is private. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction. Changing visibility may also be restricted by organization policies.

inputs.name: archived
inputs.description: Archives the repository (making it read-only) or unarchives it.

inputs.name: has_wiki
inputs.description: Enables the GitHub Wiki.

inputs.name: homepage
inputs.description: URL of the project's homepage.

inputs.name: has_issues
inputs.description: Enables GitHub Issues for this repository.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Repository visibility. IMPORTANT: Public forks cannot be made private - this is a GitHub platform restriction.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Brief summary of the repository.
examples: A library for advanced data processing.

inputs.name: is_template
inputs.description: Designates this repository as a template repository.

inputs.name: has_projects
inputs.description: Enables GitHub Projects. Note: Organization settings might override this and cause an error if projects are disabled organization-wide.

inputs.name: allow_forking
inputs.description: Allows private forks of this repository. IMPORTANT: This setting can ONLY be changed for organization-owned repositories. Attempting to set this for user-owned repositories will result in a 422 error. Omit this field if you don't want to change the forking setting.

inputs.name: default_branch
inputs.description: Name of the default branch.

inputs.name: allow_auto_merge
inputs.description: Enables auto-merge for pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_merge_commit
inputs.description: Allows merging pull requests via merge commits.

inputs.name: allow_rebase_merge
inputs.description: Allows rebase-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: allow_squash_merge
inputs.description: Allows squash-merging pull requests.

inputs.name: merge_commit_title
inputs.description: Default title for merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'MERGE_MESSAGE'. CONSTRAINT: If set to 'PR_TITLE', merge_commit_message must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. If set to 'MERGE_MESSAGE', merge_commit_message must be blank/unset.

inputs.name: allow_update_branch
inputs.description: Allows updating a pull request's head branch from the base branch if not strictly required for merging.

inputs.name: merge_commit_message
inputs.description: Default message for merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'PR_TITLE', or 'BLANK'. CONSTRAINT: If merge_commit_title is 'PR_TITLE', this must be 'PR_BODY' or 'BLANK'. Cannot be set when merge_commit_title is 'MERGE_MESSAGE'.

inputs.name: delete_branch_on_merge
inputs.description: Automatically deletes head branches after merging pull requests.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_title
inputs.description: Default title for squash merge commits: 'PR_TITLE' or 'COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE'.

inputs.name: squash_merge_commit_message
inputs.description: Default message for squash merge commits: 'PR_BODY', 'COMMIT_MESSAGES', or 'BLANK'.

inputs.name: web_commit_signoff_required
inputs.description: Requires contributors to sign off on web-based commits.

inputs.name: use_squash_pr_title_as_default
inputs.description: DEPRECATED: Use `squash_merge_commit_title`. If true, squash merge commits use PR title; otherwise, commit message.

inputs.name: security__and__analysis__secret__scanning__status
inputs.description: Manages secret scanning. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

inputs.name: security__and__analysis__advanced__security__status
inputs.description: Manages GitHub Advanced Security. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

inputs.name: security__analysis__secret__scanning__push__protection__status
inputs.description: Manages secret scanning push protection. Set to 'enabled' or 'disabled'.

name: Update a repository variable
description: Updates the name and/or value of an existing GitHub Actions variable in a repository.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The current name of the Actions variable to update (path parameter). Variable names are case-insensitive and can only contain alphanumeric characters or underscores.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension; case-insensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (e.g., GitHub username or organization name); case-insensitive.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The new value for the Actions variable. If omitted, the current value is retained. Must be a non-empty string if provided. You must provide either new_name or value (or both).

inputs.name: new_name
inputs.description: Optional new name for the variable. If provided, renames the variable. Can only contain alphanumeric characters or underscores; cannot start with GITHUB_ prefix.

name: Update a repository webhook
description: Updates the URL, content type, secret, SSL verification, events, or active status for an existing repository webhook, specified by `owner`, `repo`, and `hook_id`.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: active
inputs.description: Set `true` if active and sending notifications, `false` otherwise.

inputs.name: events
inputs.description: List of events to trigger the webhook. GitHub REPLACES the full event list when this is sent — omit (leave unset) to preserve the existing subscription. Use `add_events`/`remove_events` for incremental changes. See GitHub webhook event payloads docs for available event types.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the webhook (found in `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a delivery).

inputs.name: add_events
inputs.description: Events to add to current subscriptions.

inputs.name: config__url
inputs.description: New URL for webhook payload delivery.

inputs.name: remove_events
inputs.description: Events to remove from current subscriptions.

inputs.name: config__secret
inputs.description: Optional secret for HMAC digest of delivery signature headers (see GitHub docs).

inputs.name: config__content__type
inputs.description: Media type for serializing payloads ('json' or 'form').

inputs.name: config__insecure__ssl
inputs.description: Set to '1' to disable SSL verification (insecure), '0' to enable.

name: Update a team
description: Updates a team's settings (e.g., name, description, privacy, parent team) within a GitHub organization, identified by its slug and organization name.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization's name (case-insensitive). This is a required path parameter.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: New display name for the team.

inputs.name: privacy
inputs.description:
The desired privacy level for the team. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `privacy` intact. When a team is nested, the `privacy` for parent teams cannot be `secret`.
  - For a non-nested team:
    - `secret`: Visible only to organization owners and members of this team.
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).
  - For a parent or child team:
    - `closed`: Visible to all members of this organization.
    - `public`: Visible to all members of this organization (equivalent to `closed`).

inputs.name: team_slug
inputs.description: The team's unique slug identifier (typically URL-friendly version of team name). This is a required path parameter.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: **Deprecated**. Default permission (pull, push, or admin) for new repositories added to this team. This may be removed in future versions.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: New team description; an empty string clears the existing description.
examples: Responsible for all frontend development aspects of Project X

inputs.name: parent_team_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier (ID) of an existing team to set as this team's parent. This establishes a nested team structure. To remove an existing parent team relationship and make this a top-level team, explicitly pass `null` for this field (e.g., by setting this parameter to Python `None`). If this parameter is not provided in the request, the current parent team, if any, remains unchanged.

inputs.name: notification_setting
inputs.description:
The notification setting the team has chosen. Editing teams without specifying this parameter leaves `notification_setting` intact.
  - `notifications_enabled`: Team members receive notifications when the team is @mentioned.
  - `notifications_disabled`: No one receives notifications for team @mentions.

name: Update branch protection
description: Updates the protection settings for an existing branch in a repository, which must not contain wildcard characters.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: Name of the branch to protect; wildcard characters are not allowed (use GraphQL API for wildcard support).

inputs.name: lock_branch
inputs.description: If `True`, sets the branch to read-only, preventing pushes.

inputs.name: enforce_admins
inputs.description: Enforces configured restrictions for repository administrators. Set to `True` to enforce for admins, or `None`/`null` to disable.

inputs.name: allow_deletions
inputs.description: Allows deletion of the protected branch.

inputs.name: block_creations
inputs.description: If `True`, push restrictions also apply to creating matching new branches, unless pusher has explicit push access.

inputs.name: allow_force_pushes
inputs.description: Permits force pushes to the protected branch. Set to `True` to allow, `None`/`null` to block.

inputs.name: allow_fork_syncing
inputs.description: Allows users to pull upstream changes into their fork when the branch is locked.

inputs.name: restrictions__apps
inputs.description: App `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_users` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: restrictions__teams
inputs.description: Team `slug`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_users`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: restrictions__users
inputs.description: User `login`s with push access. Cannot be used with `restrictions_apps` or `restrictions_teams`. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required_linear_history
inputs.description: Enforces linear commit history by preventing merge commits. Repository must allow squash or rebase merging.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__checks
inputs.description: List of status checks required to merge. Each check object needs a `context` string and optionally an `app_id` integer. **Mutually exclusive with `contexts`** - use one or the other, not both.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__strict
inputs.description: Require branches to be up to date with the base branch before merging; if `True`, merges are blocked if the head branch is behind the base branch.

inputs.name: required_conversation_resolution
inputs.description: Requires all conversations on code to be resolved before merging a pull request.

inputs.name: required__status__checks__contexts
inputs.description: **Deprecated**: List of status check contexts required to merge. If set by a GitHub App, future checks must come from that app. Use `checks` for finer control. **Mutually exclusive with `checks`** - use one or the other, not both.

inputs.name: required__pull__request__reviews__dismiss__stale__reviews
inputs.description: If `True`, new commits dismiss previous review approvals on a pull request branch.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__approving__review__count
inputs.description: Number of approving reviews (0-6) required to merge a pull request; 0 disables.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__apps
inputs.description: App `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__teams
inputs.description: Team `slug`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__bypass__allowances__users
inputs.description: User `login`s allowed to bypass pull request requirements. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__apps
inputs.description: App `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__teams
inputs.description: Team `slug`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__dismiss__restrictions__users
inputs.description: User `login`s allowed to dismiss pull request reviews. Empty list removes all.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__require__code__owner__reviews
inputs.description: If `True`, pull requests affecting files with code owners must be reviewed by them.

inputs.name: required__pull_request__reviews__require__last__push__approval
inputs.description: If `True`, the most recent push must be approved by someone other than the pusher.

name: Update issue type
description: Update an existing issue type for a GitHub organization. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization administrator. OAuth tokens need admin:org scope.

inputs.name: org
inputs.description: The organization name (case-insensitive).

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the issue type.

inputs.name: color
inputs.description: The color of the issue type.

inputs.name: is_enabled
inputs.description: Whether the issue type is enabled at the organization level.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the issue type.

inputs.name: issue_type_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the issue type to update.

name: Update a review comment for a pull request
description: Updates the body of an existing review comment on a pull request.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The new text of the pull request review comment. Must be a non-empty string.
examples: This is the updated review comment text.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: comment_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the pull request review comment to be updated.

name: Update a webhook configuration for a repository
description: Updates the configuration (e.g., payload URL, content type, secret, SSL verification) for an existing webhook in a specified repository.

inputs.name: url
inputs.description: The URL to which webhook payloads will be delivered.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository (not case-sensitive).

inputs.name: secret
inputs.description: Optional secret for generating an HMAC hex digest of the payload, included in the `X-Hub-Signature-256` header to verify authenticity.

inputs.name: hook_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the webhook, typically found in the `X-GitHub-Hook-ID` header of a webhook delivery.

inputs.name: content_type
inputs.description: Media type for payload serialization (e.g., `json`, `form`). GitHub defaults to `form` if this field is not provided.

inputs.name: insecure_ssl
inputs.description: Specifies SSL certificate verification: '0' for enabled (default behavior if omitted), '1' for disabled (not recommended).

name: Update status check protection
description: Updates required status checks for a branch, optionally requiring it to be up-to-date before merging.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The name of the branch (required, cannot be null or empty). Wildcard characters are not allowed. To use wildcard characters in branch names, please use the GraphQL API.

inputs.name: checks
inputs.description: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. Each check object should have a `context` (string) and an optional `app_id` (integer). Example: `[{"context": "continuous-integration/jenkins", "app_id": 12345}]`

inputs.name: strict
inputs.description: Specifies whether to require branches to be up to date before merging.

inputs.name: contexts
inputs.description: **Deprecated**: A list of status checks to require in order to merge into this branch. If any of these checks were recently set by a particular GitHub App, they will be required to come from that app in the future for the branch to merge. For more fine-grained control, use `checks` instead of `contexts`. Can be provided as a single string or a list of strings.

name: Update Subscription
description: Tool to update notification subscription state for issues, pull requests, or discussions. Use when you need to subscribe, unsubscribe, or ignore notifications for a specific GitHub item.

inputs.name: state
inputs.description: The new subscription state. SUBSCRIBED: receive notifications for all activity. UNSUBSCRIBED: stop receiving notifications. IGNORED: never receive notifications even when mentioned.

inputs.name: subscribableId
inputs.description: The Node ID of the subscribable object (issue, pull request, discussion, etc.) to modify. This is a global GraphQL ID, not a repository-local number.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used for tracking and deduplication.

name: Update User List
description: Tool to update an existing GitHub user list. Use when you need to modify the name, description, or privacy settings of a user list.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The new name for the user list. Only provide if you want to change the name.

inputs.name: list_id
inputs.description: The unique ID of the user list to update. This is the GraphQL node ID (e.g., 'UL_kwDOCNqc1s4AbuJ8').

inputs.name: is_private
inputs.description: Whether the list should be private. Set to true to make private, false to make public. Only provide if you want to change visibility.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: The new description for the user list. Only provide if you want to change the description.
examples: An updated description for my list

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used for idempotency and tracking.

name: Update user lists for item
description: Tool to update which of the viewer's lists an item belongs to on GitHub. Use when you need to add an item (repository, issue, pull request, etc.) to specific user lists or move it between lists.

inputs.name: item_id
inputs.description: The global node ID of the item to add to the lists (e.g., 'R_kgDOKl1DMA' for a repository). This is the GraphQL global identifier returned by GitHub's API.

inputs.name: list_ids
inputs.description: The list of user list IDs that this item should belong to. The item will be added to these lists and removed from any other lists it previously belonged to.

inputs.name: client_mutation_id
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This can be used to track the request.

inputs.name: suggested_list_ids
inputs.description: Optional list of suggested list IDs to create and add this item to. These lists will be created if they don't exist.

name: Update Project V2 Item for User
description: Tool to update a field value for an item in a user's GitHub ProjectV2. Use when you need to modify a field value of an existing project item.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The value to set. Use {'text': 'value'} for text fields, {'number': 123} for number fields, {'singleSelectOptionId': 'option_id'} for single select, {'date': '2024-01-01'} for date fields, {'iterationId': 'iteration_id'} for iteration fields.

inputs.name: itemId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the project item to update (e.g., 'PVTI_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzgldG_w').

inputs.name: fieldId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the field to update (e.g., 'PVTSSF_lAHOCNqc1s4BN3oqzg8vTnA').

inputs.name: projectId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the Project (e.g., 'PVT_kwHOCNqc1s4BN3oq').

name: Change User Status
description: Tool to update your status on GitHub. Use when you need to set or clear your GitHub profile status with an emoji, message, and availability indicator.

inputs.name: emoji
inputs.description: The emoji to represent your status. Can either be a native Unicode emoji or an emoji name with colons, e.g., :rocket:, :smiley:. If omitted, any existing emoji will be cleared.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: A short description of your current status. Maximum 80 characters. If omitted, any existing message will be cleared.

inputs.name: expiresAt
inputs.description: If set, the user status will not be shown after this date. Must be a valid ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g., 2024-12-31T23:59:59Z). If omitted, the status will persist indefinitely.

inputs.name: organizationId
inputs.description: The global node ID of the organization whose members will be allowed to see the status. If omitted, the status will be publicly visible to everyone. Use this to limit status visibility to organization members only.

inputs.name: clientMutationId
inputs.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. This value will be returned in the response and can be used for request tracking or idempotency.

inputs.name: limitedAvailability
inputs.description: Whether this status should indicate you are not fully available on GitHub (e.g., you are away or busy). When true, shows a busy indicator. Defaults to false if omitted.

name: Upload a release asset
description: Tool to upload a binary asset to an existing GitHub release. Use when you need to attach files like installers or backups to a release programmatically.

inputs.name: repo
inputs.description: Repository name without the .git extension; not case sensitive.

inputs.name: asset
inputs.description: Binary file to upload as the release asset.

inputs.name: label
inputs.description: Short description for the asset; displayed instead of filename.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Account owner of the repository; not case sensitive.

inputs.name: release_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the release. The release must have a valid upload URL (i.e., not already finalized without one). Asset size must not exceed 2 GB.

inputs.name: content_type
inputs.description: Media type of the asset, for example 'application/zip'.
